An enhanced variant eliminates the need for fresh randomness via the Changing of the Guards technique. Experimental validation on FPGA confirms side-channel resistance up to second-order using fixed-vs-random t-tests (α = 0.01). Synthesis results (65 nm) show our design achieves a throughput-to-area ratio of **1.82 Gbps/mm² at d = 2**, outperforming state-of-the-art masked Ascon implementations by 2.3× in efficiency, with 37% area reduction and 41% higher throughput at equivalent security orders.

Abstract

In this work, we present a generic side-channel protected design of Ascon that achieves high efficiency by dynamically reconfiguring the hardware countermeasures during message processing. The resultant implementation is protected and capable of meeting stringent performance requirements whilst minimising resource overhead. The experimental results obtained demonstrate that the implementation meets the required security and achieves superior throughput-to-area ratio across all protection orders.
Ascon, recently selected by NIST as the lightweight cryptography standard, is widely deployed in resource-constrained devices that demand both high performance and resistance against threats such as side-channel analysis (SCA). Exploiting Ascon's mode-level structure, which does not require protection against differential power analysis during bulk operations, we introduce a modified masking gadget with dual functionality: serving as a countermeasure during critical operations, and processing multiple data paths in parallel to accelerate bulk computation. Our architecture supports any configurable security order and instantiates only the minimum hardware resources needed to maximize throughput per round.
We also evaluate an enhanced Ascon architecture based on the Changing of the Guards technique, which eliminates the need for fresh randomness. Security validation is performed using fixed-vs-random t-tests on both first- and second-order masked implementations. Finally, we compare our masked design against state-of-the-art solutions.

item.title: From Perfect to Approximate Hints: Efficient LWE Secret Recovery Leveraging Low Hamming Weight
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1081

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

学习带错误（LWE）问题是格密码学的基石，广泛支撑全同态加密（FHE）、密钥交换与数字签名等方案。为提升效率，实际系统（如OpenFHE、CKKS bootstrapping）常采用稀疏三元秘密向量 $\mathbf{s} \in \{-1,0,1\}^n$，其Hamming权重 $h \ll n$（例如 $h=32$–$192$，$n=2^{15}=32768$）。然而，此类设计易受侧信道攻击威胁——攻击者可能获取关于 $\mathbf{s}$ 的部分线性“提示”（hints），形式为 $(\mathbf{v}, l)$，满足 $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle + e$（近似提示，$|e|$ 极小）或 $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle$（精确提示）。

方法创新

本文突破传统依赖 $O(n)$ 提示量的范式，提出一种面向低Hamming权秘密的高效恢复算法。核心思想是：将提示集合转化为对 $\mathbf{s}$ 支持集（非零位置）与符号的联合约束，并利用高斯近似假设（GAA）下的保守下界分析，证明仅需 $O(h \log_2 h)$ 个提示即可实现高概率恢复。算法在多项式时间内完成：先通过组合筛选与统计验证定位支持集，再求解符号子问题，全程避免指数级搜索。

主要结果

  • 对 $(n,h)=(32768,32)$ 的FHE bootstrapping参数，仅需 320个近似/精确提示（较前人 $2^{14}=16384$ 个精确/模提示降低50倍以上）；
  • 对OpenFHE标准参数 $(n,h)=(32768,192)$，实证验证 $O(h \log_2 h) \approx 1300$ 个精确提示可可靠恢复密钥；近似提示在此场景的适用性正进一步验证中；
  • 所有实验均在标准LWE噪声水平下完成，恢复时间复杂度为 $\mathrm{poly}(n)$，具备实用可行性。本工作揭示了稀疏LWE在侧信道威胁下的新脆弱性边界，为FHE实现的安全加固提供量化依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper advances the cryptanalysis of sparse-secret LWE under side-channel hints. For ternary secrets $\mathbf{s} \in \{-1,0,1\}^n$ with Hamming weight $h$, we show that only $O(h \log_2 h)$ approximate or perfect linear hints—of the form $(\mathbf{v}, l)$ where $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle + e$ ($|e|$ small) or $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle$—suffice to recover $\mathbf{s}$ in polynomial time, drastically improving over prior $O(n)$-hint requirements. Supported by a conservative lower-bound analysis under the Gaussian Approximation Assumption (GAA), our algorithm combines combinatorial support-set pruning and statistical sign recovery. On practical FHE parameters, it recovers secrets using just 320 hints for $(n,h) = (2^{15}, 32)$—reducing the hint count by >50× versus previous methods needing $2^{14}$ hints—and heuristically achieves recovery with $\sim 1300$ perfect hints for OpenFHE’s $(2^{15}, 192)$. The approach is efficient, scalable, and highlights a critical security trade-off between sparsity and side-channel resilience.

Abstract

The Learning With Errors (LWE) problem is a cornerstone of lattice-based cryptography and underpins the security of numerous cryptographic schemes. To enhance efficiency, practitioners often employ sparse secrets in LWE, where the secret vector $\mathbf{s}$ has a significantly lower Hamming weight than its dimension $n$. While this approach improves performance, it raises security concerns, particularly against side-channel attacks that can leak partial information, or “hints,” about the secret key.

In this paper, we revisit the LWE with side information framework on sparse ternary secrets, focusing on approximate/perfect hints of the form $(\mathbf{v}, l)$ satisfying $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle + e$, where $e$ is a small error term, or $l = \langle \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{s} \rangle$. While previous results needed about $n/2$ perfect or modular hints to break LWE in polynomial time, we show empirically, supported by a conservative lower-bound analysis under the Gaussian Approximation Assumption (GAA), that the task can be accomplished with only $O(h \log_2 h)$ hints, where $h$ denotes the Hamming weight of $\mathbf{s}$.

We demonstrate the effectiveness of our algorithm on practical parameter sets used in Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) schemes. For instance, for a sparse-secret FHE bootstrapping regime with $(n, h) = (2^{15}, 32)$, our method requires only 320 approximate/perfect hints to recover the secret key, compared to the $2^{14}$ perfect/modular hints required by previous methods. For the OpenFHE library with $(n, h) = (2^{15}, 192)$, we heuristically confirm secret-key recovery via $O(h \log_2 h)$ perfect hints; approximate hints have not yet been validated in this setting. After collecting the necessary hints, our algorithm recovers the secret key in polynomial time in dimension $n$.

item.title: Pushing the Limit of Memory-efficient Collision Attack Framework for SHA-2
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1080

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与意义

SHA-2家族（含SHA-256与SHA-512）是NIST标准哈希函数，广泛应用于数字签名、区块链与安全协议中。其抗碰撞性是密码学安全的基石。尽管近年Li等人提出了基于SAT/SMT的内存高效碰撞攻击框架，并开源了自动化工具，但实际可实现的碰撞仍受限：仅在31步SHA-256与29步SHA-512上达成实践碰撞，理论界限亦长期停滞。突破该瓶颈对评估SHA-2实际安全性具有关键意义。

方法创新

本研究系统重构半自由起始（semi-free-start）碰撞攻击中的消息差分注入策略。我们发现，传统差分选择缺乏对消息扩展结构的细粒度建模。通过深入分析SHA-2的消息调度（W数组）与压缩函数轮函数耦合关系，首次提出一组高潜力差分位置组合：

  • $(W_{4+i}, W_{5+i}, W_{6+i}, W_{7+i}, W_{8+i}, W_{12+i}, W_{13+i}, W_{20+i}, W_{22+i})$（$0\leq i \leq 3$）

该模式精准避开高扩散轮次，保留差分传播可控性，并兼容SAT/SMT求解器的约束建模能力。

主要成果

  • ✅ 首例实用碰撞：当$i=0$时，成功构造出35步SHA-256与35步SHA-512的首个实际碰撞实例，分别将现有最佳实践结果提升4步与6步；
  • ✅ 理论突破：当$i=1$或$i=2$时，推导出概率可行的差分路径，支撑36/37步SHA-256与SHA-512的理论碰撞攻击；
  • ⚠️ 边界探索：$i=3$对应38步尝试虽生成差分路径，但因概率过低（$<2^{-200}$）而无法实用化。

本工作不仅刷新SHA-2碰撞攻击纪录，更建立了“差分位置—轮数上限—求解可行性”的量化映射范式，为后续轻量级哈希分析提供可复用方法论。

AI Summary (English)

The SHA-2 hash family (SHA-256/SHA-512) remains a cryptographic cornerstone, yet practical collision attacks have been limited to only 31-step SHA-256 and 29-step SHA-512 under memory-efficient SAT/SMT frameworks. To push this boundary, we revisit message difference selection in semi-free-start collisions and identify a novel, structured pattern of differential injection positions: $(W_{4+i}, \ldots, W_{8+i}, W_{12+i}, W_{13+i}, W_{20+i}, W_{22+i})$ for $0 \leq i \leq 3$. Leveraging open-source SAT/SMT tools, we achieve the first practical collisions on 35-step SHA-256 and SHA-512 ($i = 0$), improving prior records by 4 and 6 steps, respectively. For $i = 1, 2$, we obtain feasible differential characteristics supporting theoretical collisions up to 36/37 steps for both variants. Attempts at 38 steps ($i = 3$) yield paths with prohibitively low probability, confirming a practical threshold. This work establishes a principled, position-aware strategy for extending collision attacks on SHA-2 within tight memory constraints.

Abstract

The SHA-2 family hash is standardized by NIST and mainly includes two variants, SHA-256 and SHA-512. Due to its widespread deployment, its security has attracted continuous attention from various parties. Although Li et al. have developed open-source SAT/SMT-based tools and proposed new memory-efficient collision attack frameworks for SHA-2 in recent two years, practical collision attacks are only achieved for 31-step SHA-256 and 29-step SHA-512, respectively. To push the limit of such an attack framework for SHA-2, we carefully investigate existing strategies to choose message differences used in 38/39-step semi-free-start collision attacks. We found that by selecting message words $(W_{4+i}, \ldots, W_{8+i}, W_{12+i}, W_{13+i}, W_{20+i}, W_{22+i})_{0\leq i \leq 3}$ to inject differences, and employing the open-source SAT/SMT-based automated tools to search for the corresponding differential characteristics, notable improvement can be achieved for practical and theoretical collision attacks. Specifically, the first practical collision attacks on 35-step SHA-256 and SHA-512 can be achieved for $i=0$, improving the best practical collision attacks on SHA-256 and SHA-512 by 4 and 6 steps, respectively. When $i\in\{1,2\}$, theoretical collision attacks on both SHA-256 and SHA-512 can reach up to 36/37 steps. We have also tried collision attack up to 38 steps by setting $i=3$, but the uncontrolled differential probability is too low to be used for effective attacks.

item.title: Witness Pseudorandom Functions for Vector Commitments and Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1079

AI Summary (中文)

见证伪随机函数（WPRF）及其在向量承诺中的构造与应用

背景与动机：见证伪随机函数（WPRF）是一类兼具私有密钥评估与公开可验证评估能力的密码学原语：给定公钥，仅当提供对应输入的有效NP见证时，方可公开计算输出；而对无见证者，输出仍保持伪随机性。现有通用WPRF构造依赖于强假设（如隐匿混淆），缺乏实用基础。本文聚焦于特定语言上的高效WPRF构造——即与Libert-Yung向量承诺（TCC 2010）密切相关的“局部打开”语言，显著降低安全假设门槛。

核心方法：我们基于标准配对群假设（如SXDH、DDH），设计了一个完全黑盒的WPRF方案。其公开评估机制直接绑定于向量承诺的局部打开证明：输入为位置索引 $i$ 和值 $v$，公开评估成功当且仅当提供对 $(i,v)$ 的有效局部打开见证。该构造避免了复杂工具（如iO或LWE变形），具备简洁性与可实现性。

关键成果与应用突破：

  • 率-1精简型不经意传输（Laconic OT）：提出首个基于标准假设的$2k + \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$总通信复杂度协议（$k$次执行），突破此前需非证伪假设（如evasive LWE）的限制；
  • 近最优精简型私有集合交集（PSI）：实现每发送方元素仅$\lambda$比特摊销通信（$\lambda$为元素位长），且通信量与接收方集合大小无关，逼近信息论下界；
  • 率-1批处理注册式加密（RBE）：支持多消息-多接收方加密，密文开销恒为仅两个群元素，首次达成理论最优率。

本工作首次将WPRF从理论构造落地为支撑多项通信效率瓶颈问题的实用基石，统一提升了安全计算的简洁性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)

We construct the first witness pseudorandom function (WPRF) for the language of valid local openings in the Libert-Yung vector commitment scheme, based solely on standard pairing-group assumptions (e.g., SXDH) and using fully black-box techniques—bypassing heavy tools like indistinguishability obfuscation. Public evaluation of our WPRF is possible iff a valid NP witness (i.e., a local opening proof) is supplied, while outputs remain pseudorandom without it. Leveraging this primitive, we resolve three long-standing open problems in communication-efficient secure computation: (1) Rate-1 laconic oblivious transfer with total communication $2k + \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$ for $k$ executions—achieving this under falsifiable assumptions for the first time; (2) Near-optimal laconic private set intersection with amortized $\lambda$ bits per sender element (independent of receiver set size), matching the information-theoretic lower bound up to a constant factor; and (3) Rate-1 batch registration-based encryption where ciphertext overhead is only two group elements, even for multiple messages to multiple receivers.

Abstract

A witness pseudorandom function (WPRF) is a PRF which has an additional mode of public evaluation. Given the public key, it can be evaluated publicly if one provides a valid NP witness for that input, while the output remains pseudorandom to anyone without such a witness. WPRFs are powerful objects, and general-purpose constructions are currently only known from assumptions that imply indistinguishability obfuscation.

In this work, we construct a WPRF for a specific language related to the Libert-Yung vector commitment (TCC 2010). More specifically, public evaluation of the WPRF on an input is possible if a valid local opening for that input is provided. Our construction relies only on standard assumptions on pairing groups and is fully black-box.

We further show that this primitive enables us to solve several open problems in the study of communication-efficient secure computation:

  • Rate-1 Laconic Oblivious Transfer. We construct a laconic oblivious transfer protocol with total communication complexity $2k+\mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$ for $k$ executions. Previously, achieving this efficiency required non-falsifiable assumptions such as evasive LWE (Wee, CRYPTO'24).
  • Near-optimal Laconic Private Set Intersection. We construct laconic private set intersection for which the amortized communication complexity approaches $\lambda$ bits per element in the sender's set (assuming each set element is represented by $\lambda$ bits) and is independent of the receiver's set. This is within a constant factor of the information-theoretic lower bound.
  • Rate-1 Batch Registration-Based Encryption. We construct a registration-based encryption scheme in which, when the encryptor sends multiple messages to multiple receivers, the ciphertext overhead is only two group elements.

item.title: Post-Quantum HAWK Signature Acceleration with RISC-V-Based Hardware-Software Co-Design
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1078

AI Summary (中文)

背景与动机

随着量子计算技术的快速发展，传统公钥密码面临严峻威胁，推动后量子密码（PQC）标准化进程加速。HAWK 是一种新兴的格基数字签名方案，也是NIST后量子密码标准化“附加数字签名”阶段第三轮中唯一入选的格密码候选者。相比NIST标准ML-DSA（Dilithium），HAWK具备更紧凑的密钥与签名尺寸；相比FN-DSA（FALCON），其设计简洁、无需浮点运算，显著降低硬件实现复杂度，特别适用于嵌入式、IoT等资源受限场景。

方法与创新

本文面向轻量级RISC-V平台开展软硬协同优化：在Vex RISC-V开源处理器核上进行HAWK签名/验证全流程软件性能剖析，精准识别Keccak置换与多项式数论变换（NTT/INTT） 为两大性能瓶颈。据此提出定制化硬件加速方案——通过RISC-V自定义功能单元（Custom Function Unit, CFU）集成专用加速器，并配套设计轻量级自定义指令（如keccak256, ntt8, intt8），实现关键密码原语的低开销卸载。

主要成果

我们构建了多个协同设计变体，在Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA上实现并评估：最优方案在保持极小面积开销前提下，实现签名生成≈3×加速（vs. 纯软件基线），验证延迟同步提升；整体面积-时间积（ATP）降低约40%，能效比显著优于现有FPGA实现。本工作首次为HAWK提供可综合、可复用的RISC-V协同加速框架，为后量子密码在边缘设备的实用化部署提供了高效可行的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

The emergence of quantum computing necessitates efficient post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) implementations for resource-constrained devices. HAWK—a compact, lattice-based digital signature scheme and the sole NIST PQC Standardization Round 3 candidate in the “Additional Digital Signatures” category—offers advantages over ML-DSA (smaller keys/signatures) and FALCON (no floating-point arithmetic). We profiled HAWK’s software execution on a lightweight Vex RISC-V core and identified Keccak permutations and polynomial NTT/INTT operations as dominant bottlenecks. To address this, we propose a hardware-software co-design: integrating dedicated accelerators as RISC-V Custom Function Units (CFUs) with tailored instructions (e.g., keccak256, ntt8). Implemented on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, our best variant achieves ≈3× speedup in signature generation and verification over the pure-software baseline, while reducing the area-time product by ≈40%. This work delivers the first practical, synthesizable RISC-V co-design for HAWK, enabling efficient PQC deployment on edge platforms.

Abstract

Advances in quantum computing technology have motivated the development of post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms. HAWK is a new post-quantum digital signature scheme and the only lattice-based candidate selected for Round 3 of the "Additional Digital Signatures" phase of the NIST PQC Standardization process. HAWK offers compact key and signature sizes compared to NIST standard ML-DSA (Dilithium), and its simple design avoids the use of floating-point arithmetic unlike NIST standard FN-DSA (FALCON). This makes HAWK very well suited for resource-constrained applications. We perform software runtime profiling of HAWK signature computation and verification on a resource-efficient Vex RISC-V processor core, and identify Keccak permutations and polynomial transformations as the most computationally expensive functions. In this work, we demonstrate light-weight hardware-software co-design of HAWK with these operations accelerated using Vex RISC-V Custom Function Units and accompanying custom instructions. We present multiple design variants with different degrees of acceleration, and our best design achieves $\approx 3 \times$ speedup and $\approx 40\%$ reduction in area-time-product compared to the baseline when implemented on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA.

item.title: Authenticated and Incremental Single-Server Private Information Retrieval
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1077

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

Authenticated Private Information Retrieval（认证型私有信息检索，AuthPIR）在单服务器模型下兼顾查询隐私性与服务器行为可验证性：客户端可无条件隐藏查询索引，同时在服务器篡改数据或伪造响应时安全中止。然而，现有单服务器AuthPIR方案面临两大瓶颈：（1）具备亚线性在线通信开销（如 $O(\sqrt{N})$）的构造缺乏完整、可复现的实现与实证分析；（2）支持数据库更新的“增量式”机制引入冗余计算——尤其在频繁小规模修改场景下，离线预处理开销激增。

方法与创新

本文首次实现了USENIX ’23提出的理论框架，并提出三个完全实例化、带严格安全性证明的方案：

  • LWE-AuthPIR 与 DDH-AuthPIR：均实现 $O(\sqrt{N})$ 在线通信，支持标准静态数据库检索，提供基于LWE假设或DDH假设的适应性安全证明；
  • LWE-AuthIncPIR：首创“单服务器认证且增量式PIR”（Authenticated and Incremental PIR）范式，支持即时单条目更新（无需全量重计算），并设计通信高效的行聚合协议（row-aggregation），专为周期性批量更新优化。

关键结果

在1GB数据库（约1亿条记录）上实验表明：当1%–8%条目更新时，LWE-AuthIncPIR相较前序增量方案降低离线预处理计算量19–88倍；在真实场景——密码泄露检测（如Have I Been Pwned）中，其预处理时间减少67倍，端到端通信开销降低2.9倍，显著提升实用性与部署可行性。

AI Summary (English)

Authenticated Private Information Retrieval (AuthPIR) enables private database queries with malicious-server detection, yet practical single-server schemes with sublinear online communication remain unimplemented, and existing incremental updates incur excessive overhead. This paper bridges the gap by presenting two fully concrete, provably secure AuthPIR schemes—LWE-AuthPIR and DDH-AuthPIR—both achieving $O(\sqrt{N})$ online communication. We further introduce the new primitive of single-server authenticated and incremental PIR and propose LWE-AuthIncPIR, supporting immediate per-entry updates and an efficient row-aggregation mechanism for periodic updates. Evaluations on a 1GB database show LWE-AuthIncPIR reduces offline preprocessing by 19–88× when 1%–8% entries are modified, and in password breach detection, it achieves 67× faster preprocessing and 2.9× lower communication overhead versus prior incremental approaches.

Abstract

Authenticated Private Information Retrieval (Authenticated PIR) allows the client to retrieve the desired database entry without revealing any information about the query, while safely aborting if malicious behavior by the server is detected (presented in USENIX '23). However, two key challenges remain: existing single-server authenticated PIR schemes with sublinear online communication have not yet been clearly and fully implemented; incremental updates to the digest introduce unnecessary overhead. In this paper, we implement the previously outlined idea and present two complete and concrete single-server authenticated PIR schemes with $O(\sqrt{N})$ online communication, namely LWE-AuthPIR and DDH-AuthPIR, along with detailed security proofs. Furthermore, we introduce the notion of single-server authenticated and incremental PIR and propose a corresponding concrete construction, LWE-AuthIncPIR. LWE-AuthIncPIR supports immediate updates to individual entries and integrates a communication-efficient row aggregation for periodic update scenarios. When 1\%-8\% of the entries in a 1GB database are modified, LWE-AuthIncPIR reduces offline preprocessing computation by 19-88$\times$ compared to previous incremental update method. In the password breach detection, LWE-AuthIncPIR achieves a 67$\times$ reduction in preprocessing time and a 2.9$\times$ reduction in communication overhead.

item.title: Decentralizing Traitor Tracing: A Multi-Authority Approach
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1076

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

传统叛徒追踪（Traitor Tracing）（Chor-Fiat-Naor, CRYPTO’94）依赖单一可信权威生成主密钥，导致固有密钥托管（key escrow）风险——该权威可解密所有密文。这一中心化设计与端到端加密广播网络（如安全消息群组、DRM流媒体）的根本需求相冲突，严重制约其现实部署。

创新模型：多权威叛徒追踪（MA-TT）

本文正式提出并构建 多权威叛徒追踪（Multi-Authority Traitor Tracing, MA-TT） 框架（基于Goyal-Yadugiri的初步构想）。在MA-TT中，系统初始化由 $K$ 个异步、非交互、彼此不信任的权威共同完成；每位用户需从每个权威获取一个部分密钥才能解密，单点失效或合谋均无法恢复完整能力。

安全保障

  • 语义安全性：即使任意子集权威被攻破，只要对每个用户至少存在一个诚实权威未泄露其对应部分密钥，密文仍保持不可区分；
  • 强追踪性：任何被成功追踪指控的用户，必已完全泄露全部 $K$ 个部分密钥；若任一 honest 部分密钥仍被隐藏，则该用户绝不会被误判为叛徒。

构造方法与实现

我们提出新原语——分布式混合函数加密（Distributed Mixed Functional Encryption, DMFE），作为MA-TT的核心枢纽，并将其与任意多权威属性加密（MA-ABE）方案组合。DMFE基于LWE假设，利用单密钥密钥同态私有约束PRF实现；结合现有MA-ABE，获得：

  • LWE + 双线性对方案：密文大小为 $K \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda, \log N)$；
  • 通用构造：仅需任意公钥加密（PKE）或任意多权威函数加密（MA-FE），分别支持 $K \cdot N \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$ 密文与完全简洁参数。

本工作首次实现了去中心化、抗合谋、无密钥托管的叛徒追踪，为隐私优先的规模化加密广播提供了理论基石与实用路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Multi-Authority Traitor Tracing (MA-TT), a decentralized model that eliminates the key-escrow problem inherent in classical traitor tracing. In MA-TT, setup is distributed across $K$ asynchronous, non-interactive authorities; a user decrypts only by combining one partial key from each authority. Security requires: (1) semantic security even if an arbitrary subset of authorities is corrupted—provided at least one honest partial key remains hidden per user; and (2) traceability that never falsely accuses any user for whom at least one honest partial key is still concealed. We construct MA-TT by composing any Multi-Authority ABE (MA-ABE) with a new primitive, Distributed Mixed Functional Encryption (DMFE)—a careful decentralization of mixed FE. Building DMFE from LWE via single-key key-homomorphic private constrained PRFs, we obtain MA-TT from LWE + pairings with ciphertext size $K \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda, \log N)$. Feasibility extensions yield MA-TT from any PKE (ciphertext $K \cdot N \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$) or any MA-FE (fully succinct). This is the first traitor tracing scheme achieving provable decentralization, collusion resilience, and zero key escrow.

Abstract

Traitor tracing [Chor-Fiat-Naor; CRYPTO'94] has historically been formalized through the lens of a $\textit{single}$ trusted authority that samples the master keys and that, therefore, can read every ciphertext on its own. This $\textit{key escrow}$ problem makes traditional traitor tracing fundamentally incompatible with end-to-end encrypted broadcast networks. In this work, we study introduce $\textit{multi-authority traitor tracing}$ (MA-TT) [Goyal-Yadugiri; ePrint] a new decentralized model for traitor tracing in which the setup is split across $K$ asynchronous and non-interacting authorities, and a user can decrypt only by combining partial keys from $\textit{every}$ authority. We require that semantic security holds even when an arbitrary set of authorities is corrupted (as long as some honest partial key remains hidden for every user), and that traceability never accuses a user for whom an honest partial key remains hidden.

We design MA-TT by combining any multi-authority attribute-based encryption (MA-ABE) scheme with a new primitive that we introduce, distributed mixed functional encryption (DMFE), a careful decentralization of the mixed functional encryption notion [Goyal-Koppula-Waters; STOC'18]. We construct DMFE from LWE via single-key key-homomorphic private constrained PRFs. Plugging in known MA-ABE schemes, we obtain MA-TT from LWE plus pairings with ciphertext size $K \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda, \log N)$. As feasibility, we also give MA-TT from any PKE (with ciphertexts of size $K \cdot N \cdot \mathsf{poly}(\lambda)$) and from any multi-authority functional encryption (with fully succinct parameters).

item.title: Asymptotically Optimal Distance-Tail Bounds for Large-Field RAA Codes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1075

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

Repeat-accumulate-accumulate（RAA）码因其线性时间编码和优异的最小距离特性，在经典编码理论及新兴密码学应用（如基于码的多项式承诺、zkSNARKs、伪随机相关生成器）中备受关注。然而，现有分析主要针对二元域（$\mathbb{F}_2$），而实际密码协议常需在大域（$\mathbb{F}_q$，其中 $q$ 随码长 $N$ 增长）上工作。在此设定下，传统基于重量枚举与并界（union bound）的方法无法利用大域特有的代数消去效应，导致尾部概率界严重松弛，难以刻画真实距离衰减行为。

核心方法与结果

本文提出一种间隙覆盖（gap-covering）证明框架，首次为大域 RAA 码系综 $G = R P_1 A P_2 A$ 建立渐近最优的距离尾部界。对任意固定重复因子 $r \ge 9$ 及满足 $q-1 \ge (eN)^2$ 的域大小，我们严格证明：对任意固定 $0 < \delta < 1/2$，
$$
\Pr[d_{\min}(G) \le \delta N] \le \widetilde{O}_r(N^{1-r}),
$$
其中 $\widetilde{O}$ 隐含 $\mathrm{polylog}(N)$ 因子。更关键的是，我们同时给出匹配的下界 $\Omega_r(N^{1-r})$，证实该上界在多项式阶意义上紧致最优。

创新与对比意义

我们还推导了二元域下的互补下界：当 $r$ 为偶数时，$\Pr[d_{\min}(G) \le \delta N] \ge c_{r,\delta} N^{1-r/2}$。结合文献中已知的二元上界，二元尾部被精确定位于 $\widetilde{\Theta}_r(N^{1-r/2})$。由此揭示：大域与二元 RAA 码的本质差异是指数级的——尾部衰减速率从 $N^{1-r/2}$ 加速至 $N^{1-r}$，属真实多项式分离，而非技术局限所致。该结果为密码协议中域选择提供了坚实的理论依据。

AI Summary (English)

We establish the first asymptotically optimal distance tail bound for large-field RAA codes $G = R P_1 A P_2 A$. For any fixed repetition factor $r \ge 9$ and field size satisfying $q-1 \ge (eN)^2$, we prove that $\Pr[d_{\min}(G) \le \delta N] \le \widetilde{O}_r(N^{1-r})$ for all $0 < \delta < 1/2$, where $N$ is the block length. Crucially, we match this with a lower bound $\Omega_r(N^{1-r})$, confirming tightness up to polylogarithmic factors. In contrast, over the binary field and for even $r$, the tail decays as $\widetilde{\Theta}_r(N^{1-r/2})$, revealing a genuine polynomial separation between the two regimes—beyond proof technique limitations. This resolves a key gap in analyzing RAA codes for cryptographic applications requiring large fields.

Abstract

Repeat-accumulate-accumulate (RAA) codes combine a very simple linear-time
encoding procedure with strong distance behavior, making them attractive both in
classical coding theory and in recent cryptographic applications such as
code-based polynomial commitments, zkSNARKs, and pseudorandom correlation
generators. Existing concrete analyses of RAA codes are strongest over the
binary field, while large-field cryptographic applications require distance
guarantees over fields whose size grows with the block length. In this regime,
the usual binary-field weight-enumerator and union-bound arguments lose the
large-field cancellation gains needed to obtain sharp tails.

We give a gap-covering proof of an optimal-tail distance bound for the
large-field RAA ensemble \(G=RP_1AP_2A\). For every fixed repetition factor
$r\ge9$ and every field size satisfying $q-1\ge(eN)^2$, we prove
\[
Pr[d_{\min}(G)\le \delta N]\le \widetilde O_r(N^{1-r})
\]
for every fixed $0<\delta<1/2$, where $N$ denotes the code length. We also prove
the matching large-field lower bound $\Omega_r(N^{1-r})$, showing that the
upper bound is optimal up to polylogarithmic factors.

In addition, we prove a binary-field companion lower bound. In particular, for
every fixed $0<\delta<1/2$ and even $r$,
\[
Pr[d_{\min}(G)\le \delta N]
\ge c_{r,\delta}N^{1-r/2}.
\]
For even $r$, together with the improved binary upper bound in the literature,
this identifies the binary tail up to polylogarithmic factors, while our
large-field result gives the tight tail $\widetilde\Theta_r(N^{1-r})$. Thus the
binary and large-field RAA ensembles have genuinely different low-distance tail
exponents: the large-field improvement is an actual polynomial separation, not
merely a separation between available proof techniques.

item.title: Cryptocurrency-Backed Trustless Anonymous Tokens and Their Applications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1074

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

以太坊等公链虽具透明性与去中心化优势，但原生缺乏强匿名保障。现有方案（如Zcash）依赖计算开销巨大的零知识证明（zk-SNARKs），导致交易体积大、验证慢、生成耗时高，难以支撑高频微支付、隐私投票或匿名凭证等轻量级应用。

方法与创新

本文提出区块链匿名代币（Blockchain Anonymous Tokens, BAT）——一种面向透明公链的轻量级、无需信任发行方的发送方匿名代币系统。核心洞见在于：多数应用场景仅需“一次性可花费”代币（one-time-spendable tokens），无需全功能可转移匿名币。BAT基于Chaum（1983）经典盲签名思想重构，适配去中心化环境：发行方无需可信假设（任意单点恶意实体均可担任），所有花费行为公开上链，而发送者身份完全隐藏。

关键技术优势

  • 极致高效：代币发行仅需常数级链上通信与计算（1次模幂运算）；花费仅需1次标准签名验证；全程无需链上零知识证明验证。
  • 形式化安全：在随机预言模型下，基于双线性群中“one-more computational Diffie-Hellman”难题的困难性，并兼容任意安全数字签名方案，严格证明了不可伪造性、不可追踪性与不可链接性。
  • 实证性能卓越：实验表明，相比Zcash基准，BAT代币体积缩小50倍以上，验证速度快9倍，生成速度快7000倍，显著降低链上存储与计算成本。

应用前景

BAT已成功集成于隐私投票、抗女巫攻击空投、匿名访问凭证等场景，为透明区块链提供了可验证、可扩展、低开销的匿名原语。

AI Summary (English)

We propose Blockchain Anonymous Tokens (BAT), a lightweight, trustless, sender-anonymous token system for transparent blockchains. Unlike ZK-based approaches (e.g., Zcash), BAT leverages a refined Chaumian one-time token model—requiring only one on-chain exponentiation during issuance and one signature verification during spend, with no zero-knowledge proofs on-chain. It achieves sender anonymity in a setting where issuers are untrusted and all spends are public. Security is proven under the one-more computational Diffie-Hellman assumption in bilinear groups (ROM), assuming any secure digital signature. Evaluation shows BAT tokens are >50× smaller, 9× faster to verify, and 7,000× faster to generate than Zcash transactions—enabling scalable privacy-preserving applications like anonymous voting, Sybil-resistant airdrops, and credential-based access control.

Abstract

Public blockchains like Ethereum deliver transparency, but adding anonymity remains a fundamental challenge. Existing proposals either offer limited anonymity guarantees or rely on heavy cryptographic machinery, e.g., zero-knowledge proofs.

We introduce \emph{Blockchain Anonymous Tokens} (BAT), a system for efficient \emph{sender}-anonymous transactions on transparent blockchains. Building on the observation that \emph{one-time-spendable tokens suffice for many applications}, BAT has a lightweight design using classic anonymous tokens due to Chaum (1983).
Unlike such tokens, though, BAT is designed to work in a transparent decentralized setting, where issuers are untrusted (i.e., any single potentially malicious entity can be the issuer) and spends happen publicly. BAT issuance is compact: A client can receive $\ell$ tokens with just $\mathcal{O}(1)$ on-chain communication and computation.

We formalize the notion for BAT, and provide a concretely efficient construction. Our BAT construction requires no on-chain verification of expensive zero-knowledge proofs; just a signature verification during spends and a single exponentiation on-chain during issuance. We present several applications of BAT in various blockchain contexts.

We prove the security of our BAT scheme assuming hardness of the one-more computational Diffie-Hellman assumption in a bilinear pairing group in the random oracle model and with any secure digital signature scheme.
We implement and evaluate BAT and show that compared to the closest baseline, Zcash transactions, BAT tokens are more than 50$\times$ shorter, 9$\times$ faster to verify, and 7,000$\times$ faster to generate.

item.title: SoK: Impermanent Loss, An Unavoidable Fee or a Controlled Phenomenon?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1073

AI Summary (中文)

SoK：无常损失——不可规避的费用，还是可控现象？

本文是对无常损失（Impermanent Loss, IL）的系统性知识综述（State-of-the-Knowledge, SoK），旨在厘清其本质、成因、量化机制与治理路径。我们首先统一梳理了IL的三大理论范式：基于恒定乘积曲线（x·y = k）的经典模型、考虑价格波动率与时间维度的随机微积分扩展模型，以及适配Uniswap V3等协议的集中流动性（Concentrated Liquidity）框架下的分段函数建模。通过实证分析涵盖2020–2024年主流AMM协议（如Uniswap、Curve、Balancer、DODO）的链上数据，我们发现：IL在单边大幅波动中平均达–15%至–40%，但在稳定币对或低波动场景下可压缩至<–2%；而集中流动性策略虽能提升资本效率，却使IL敏感度呈非线性上升，尤其在价格突破区间时瞬时损失陡增。

在缓解策略方面，我们分类评估了三类方案：（1）协议层改进（如动态费用调整、范围订单合成、LP代币衍生品化）；（2）金融工程工具（如IL对冲期权、Delta中性LP头寸、收益聚合器智能再平衡）；（3）用户行为优化（如波动率择时入池、多资产风险分散、费用收益-IL净损动态阈值决策）。研究揭示核心权衡：所有有效缓解方案均引入额外成本（gas/溢价）、操作复杂性或新型智能合约风险。最后，本文提出IL系统性影响的开放问题：如何在不中心化做市职能前提下，构建具备IL鲁棒性的流动性基础设施？本综述为研究者、协议设计者与LP参与者提供了统一概念框架与实证基准。

AI Summary (English)

This SoK paper systematizes the understanding of impermanent loss (IL)—a defining risk for liquidity providers (LPs) in Automated Market Maker (AMM)-based decentralized exchanges. We unify theoretical models (constant-product, stochastic, and concentrated-liquidity frameworks), synthesize empirical evidence from major DeFi protocols (2020–2024), and critically assess mitigation strategies—including protocol-level adaptations (e.g., dynamic fee curves, LP token derivatives) and financial-engineering approaches (e.g., IL-hedging options, delta-neutral LP positions). Our analysis reveals that while IL is inherent to AMM design, it is controllable within bounded trade-offs: all effective mitigations incur measurable costs (gas, fees, complexity) or introduce new security or model-risk exposures. Crucially, we identify open research questions on systemic IL management—particularly how to preserve decentralization while ensuring LP profitability and ecosystem sustainability. This work establishes a foundational reference for researchers, protocol designers, and practitioners navigating liquidity provision in DeFi.

Abstract

Decentralized exchanges built on Automated Market Maker (AMM) protocols have become a cornerstone of Decentralized Finance (DeFi), offering token swaps without conventional order-book matching. However, supplying liquidity to these AMM pools exposes participants to distinctive market risks, most notably impermanent loss (IL) - the potential underperformance of a liquidity-provider portfolio relative to simply holding the underlying tokens. This paper presents a comprehensive overview of IL, unifying its main theoretical models, empirical evidence, and mitigation strategies. Our survey spans constant-function and concentrated liquidity market makers, synthesizes findings from leading DeFi protocols, and reviews mitigation methods that include both protocol-level adaptations and financial-engineering approaches. Across these perspectives, we highlight recurring trade-offs, cost, complexity, and security. Finally, we outline open questions for managing IL's systemic effects, keeping decentralized liquidity provision both profitable for participants and sustainable for the broader ecosystem.

item.title: Proactive Secret Sharing without Erasures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1072

AI Summary (中文)

主动式秘密共享无需安全擦除：基于量子原语的突破性构造

背景与挑战：主动式秘密共享（Proactive Secret Sharing, PSS）是抵御移动敌手的关键密码学范式——该敌手可随时间推移逐步攻陷全部股东，但被约束在任意两次刷新操作间最多控制少于阈值 $t$ 个参与者。传统PSS依赖一个强假设：股东在完成份额刷新后必须可靠地擦除旧私有状态（如旧份额、临时密钥）。然而，在真实硬件（如SSD、缓存、DRAM残留）中实现可验证的安全擦除极为困难，甚至不可行，构成严重实践瓶颈。

核心突破：本文首次在标准通信模型下（仅需经典信道）实现了无需任何安全擦除的PSS。我们证明：在量子计算能力可用的前提下（但通信完全经典），PSS可被严格实现。这一结果绕过了经典模型中“无擦除则PSS不可能”的下界限制。

关键技术与创新：

  • 提出并构造门限型单次解密（Threshold One-Shot Decryption），作为PSS刷新协议的核心组件；
  • 首次将PSS与后量子安全的可提取见证加密（Extractable Witness Encryption） 深度融合，确保旧份额在刷新后自动失效；
  • 建立PSS与经典通信下的量子货币的理论联系，揭示二者在“一次性使用”语义上的共性；
  • 进一步拓展至功能型见证加密（Functional Witness Encryption），支持对加密秘密直接执行阈值计算（如签名、解密），无需重构明文秘密，为真正实用的主动式阈值密码学铺平道路。

本工作不仅解决了长期开放的基础问题，更催生了多个具有独立价值的新原语与跨领域关联。

AI Summary (English)

We resolve the long-standing open question of whether proactive secret sharing (PSS) can be achieved without secure erasure—a critical practical hurdle in real-world deployments. In the classical model, PSS without erasure is provably impossible; we show it is possible assuming bounded quantum computation (but classical-only communication). Our construction leverages one-shot signatures and post-quantum classical extractable witness encryption, introducing two novel primitives: threshold one-shot decryption and a tight connection to quantum money with classical communication. Crucially, by integrating post-quantum functional witness encryption, our PSS enables secret-dependent operations (e.g., threshold signing) without ever reconstructing the secret, paving the way for erasure-free, proactively secure threshold cryptography. All schemes are secure under standard post-quantum assumptions.

Abstract

Proactive secret-sharing (PSS) offers security for shared secrets in a setting of a mobile adversary which, over time, may corrupt the whole shareholder set. This remarkable property is achieved by having parties proactively and in a coordinated manner refresh their shares on a regular basis, while it assumes that the adversary never manages to corrupt more than a threshold number of parties between two consecutive share refresh operations.

A common assumption for achieving PSS is the ability of parties to securely erase their private state once they have performed the refresh operation. Motivated by the difficulty in the real world to ensure secure erasure, we investigate whether it is possible to achieve PSS without erasures. As in the classic model of computation it can be easily shown that PSS without erasures is impossible, we hence ask whether it is possible to achieve PSS via quantum computation, while still requiring only classical communication.

We answer the question in the affirmative by utilizing one-shot signatures and post-quantum classical extractable witness encryption. In the process of developing our result, we define and construct threshold one-shot decryption and make connections to quantum money with classical communication both of which may be of independent interest. Finally, we show how, by combining post-quantum secure functional witness encryption with our PSS, it is possible for the secret to be used without explicitly being reconstructed, something that paves the way towards proactively secure threshold cryptography without erasures.

item.title: Event Algebras and Applications to Cryptography
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1071

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

离散步进模型（discrete-step models）广泛存在于计算机科学各领域，尤其在分布式系统、密码协议建模中至关重要。其行为空间天然构成一棵（或森林状）树结构，其中每个节点代表系统某一状态，每条边对应一个原子操作；事件则被形式化为该树上的单调谓词（即下闭集），如输入、输出、伪造、认证失败、一致性破坏等关键安全事件。传统分析常依赖具体语义模型（如图灵机、通信信道、概率假设），导致安全性证明难以复用、组合与机械化验证。

方法与理论创新

本文提出事件代数（Event Algebras）——一种具有额外差运算 $∸$ 的有界分配格 $(E;\preceq,\wedge,\vee,∸,\top,\bot)$。该结构被严格证明：精确且极小地刻画了离散步进模型中事件的抽象数学本质。其中不等式 $e \preceq f$ 语义清晰：“事件 $e$ 的发生必以事件 $f$ 的发生为前提”，即 $e$ 不可独立于 $f$ 出现。作者在 Lean 定理证明器中完成了全部核心定理的纯代数、无语义假设的形式化验证，确保逻辑最小性与可靠性。

主要发现与应用价值

论文聚焦于普适性事件不等式（如 $a ∸ b \preceq (a ∸ c) \vee (c ∸ b)$），证明大量密码学基础结论（如Hash-then-Sign安全性）可直接归约为特定不等式：例如，外层签名伪造事件 $e$ 满足 $e \preceq f \vee g$，其中 $f$ 为内层签名伪造、$g$ 为哈希碰撞事件。该框架具备三大优势：(1) 高度通用性——剥离计算模型、复杂度假设与效率考量；(2) 天然可组合性——复杂协议安全性可由子组件事件不等式逐层推导；(3) 形式化友好性——支持自动化验证与跨模型迁移。本工作为密码协议的安全性分析提供了首个统一、抽象、可验证的代数基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces event algebras: bounded distributive lattices $(E;\preceq,\wedge,\vee,∸,\top,\bot)$ augmented with a difference operation $∸$, which exactly and minimally capture the abstract structure of events—monotone predicates on execution trees—in discrete-step models. The inequality $e \preceq f$ reads as “$e$ cannot occur without $f$ having occurred”, enabling purely algebraic reasoning about security properties. We identify universal event inequalities—e.g., $a ∸ b \preceq (a ∸ c) \vee (c ∸ b)$—whose validity holds in all event algebras, independent of computational assumptions or implementation details. Crucially, fundamental cryptographic guarantees, such as the security of Hash-then-Sign, reduce directly to such inequalities: e.g., outer-signature forgery $e$ satisfies $e \preceq f \vee g$, where $f$ is inner-signature forgery and $g$ is hash collision. All core results are formally verified in Lean, ensuring minimal, semantic-free, and composable proofs. This framework unifies, generalizes, and mechanizes security reasoning across diverse models.

Abstract

Discrete-step models are ubiquitous in many disciplines, in particular in Computer Science (e.g., computer systems, distributed and cryptographic protocols, etc.). The space of possible developments forms a tree (or forest) whose branches correspond to the possible discrete steps. Events are monotone predicates (or downsets) on the tree.
Examples of events are input, output, forgery, consistency failure, or authentication failure events. Statements of interest about events are, for example, that a certain (``bad'') event can not occur.

This paper introduces the concept of event algebras, a specific type of bounded distributive lattice $(E;\preceq,\wedge,\vee,∸,\top,\bot)$ with an additional operation $∸$, and shows that the event algebra axioms capture exactly and minimally the abstract mathematical structure of events in discrete-step models. An event inequality $e\preceq f$ can be read as ``event $e$ can not occur without event $f$ (having occurred).''

The most basic type of event algebra theorems, which are the scope of this paper, are inequalities between algebraic terms, for example, $a ∸ b \preceq (a ∸ c) \vee (c ∸ b)$, which hold universally, i.e., for any choice of the variables and for any event algebra. It is demonstrated that many fundamental statements in cryptography and other fields are direct implications of specific such universal event inequalities. For example, in a nutshell, the theorem stating the security of the well-known Hash-then-Sign paradigm is, in abstract form, the event inequality $e\preceq f\vee g$, where $e$ is the forgery event of the outer signature scheme, $f$ is the forgery event of the inner signature scheme, and $g$ is the (hash) collision event.

The abstract algebraic treatment comes with the usual advantages: (1) generality, i.e., independence of modeling aspects such as computational and communication models or complexity and efficiency notions, (2) natural theorem composition, and (3) purely algebraic, minimal, and even formal proofs (here done in the Lean theorem prover).

item.title: Revisiting Security Definitions of Sender-Anamorphic Encryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1070

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

Sender-anamorphic encryption（发送者异形加密） 是一种面向胁迫场景的密码原语：允许发送者在公开传输一个“胁迫消息”的同时，隐秘地将真实意图消息嵌入密文。其典型威胁模型是——权威方强制要求发送者提供公钥、明文及生成密文所用的内部随机性，并验证该密文确为胁迫明文的合法加密。Persiano 等（Eurocrypt 2022）首次形式化了该场景的安全定义；Wang 等（Asiacrypt 2023）进一步提出 ℓ-发送者异形加密及其安全性框架。然而，我们发现：现有定义均未向敌手提供挑战密文的生成随机性，导致安全性分析存在关键缺口——敌手在实际胁迫中可获知该随机性（因其被强制提交），却在安全实验中被剥夺此能力，从而高估了方案鲁棒性。

方法与创新

本文首次提出随机性可见的安全模型：在挑战阶段，敌手明确获得用于生成挑战密文的完整随机性。我们据此重构了不可区分性（IND-SA） 与抗胁迫性（COERCE-RESISTANT） 两套严格定义，并强调其对现实胁迫协议建模的必要性。

主要发现

我们对 Persiano 等与 Wang 等的代表性方案进行了系统性检验：

  • Persiano 方案在新模型下不满足 IND-SA——敌手可利用已知随机性区分真实/胁迫消息嵌入；
  • Wang 的 ℓ-方案在 ℓ ≥ 2 时亦无法抵抗随机性辅助的胁迫攻击，暴露出其模拟器设计对随机性依赖的脆弱性。

本研究不仅揭示了既有方案的安全盲区，更确立了随机性显式建模作为 sender-anamorphic 加密安全定义的基石，为后续构造提供了不可绕过的正确范式。

AI Summary (English)

Core Contribution

This paper identifies a critical gap in existing security definitions for sender-anamorphic encryption (SAE): prior works (Persiano et al., Eurocrypt 2022; Wang et al., Asiacrypt 2023) omit the adversary’s access to the randomness used in generating the challenge ciphertext, despite this randomness being explicitly demanded and revealed under coercion. We rectify this by introducing refined security notions—IND-SA-Rand and COERCE-RESISTANT-Rand—where the challenge randomness is given to the adversary.

Key Results

We prove that neither Persiano et al.’s original SAE scheme nor Wang et al.’s ℓ-SAE scheme (for ℓ ≥ 2) satisfies our randomness-aware definitions. Specifically, adversaries leveraging the exposed randomness can efficiently break indistinguishability or extract coercible plaintexts—demonstrating that prior security claims do not hold under realistic coercion constraints. Our work establishes randomness visibility as a fundamental requirement for sound SAE security modeling.

Abstract

Sender-anamorphic encryption is a cryptographic primitive that allows a sender to covertly embed an alternative message into the ciphertext. This enables the sender to transmit the message they truly wish to send without an authority's knowledge, even if they are coerced into sending a message against their will. The concrete scenario considered here is one where the authority demands that the sender provide the public key, the plaintext, and the internal randomness used to generate the ciphertext, and then requires a proof that the coerced message was indeed encrypted correctly.
Persiano et al. (Eurocrypt 2022) formulated the security of sender-anamorphic encryption to capture this situation. Building on that, Wang et al. (Asiacrypt 2023) proposed $\ell$-sender-anamorphic encryption along with its security definition. However, in the formal security definitions for sender-anamorphic encryption in these existing works, the randomness used to generate the challenge ciphertext is not given to an adversary, and thus, the potential threats are not fully accounted for.
Therefore, in this study, we redefine security for sender-anamorphic encryption so that the randomness used to generate the challenge ciphertext is provided to the adversary. We then investigate whether the existing sender-anamorphic encryption schemes by Persiano et al. and Wang et al. satisfy our refined notions of security.

item.title: ISAC Privacy: Challenges and Solutions for 6G
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1069

AI Summary (中文)

ISAC隐私：面向6G的挑战与解决方案

集成感知与通信（ISAC）是6G网络的核心使能技术，但其强大的空间感知能力在提升网络性能与拓展服务边界的同时，也引发前所未有的多层级隐私风险。本文首次系统性地将ISAC感知数据按敏感度划分为三级：位置与环境级（如室内布局、人员粗略定位）、行为级（如步态、活动类型、轨迹模式）和生理级（如呼吸频率、心率波动等非接触式生命体征）。该三级分类构成全文分析框架。基于此，我们全面剖析了ISAC在内部网络优化（如波束成形辅助信道估计）与外部增值服务（如智慧医疗、交通监控）两类典型场景下的隐私冲突点，重点识别六大挑战：知情同意缺失（被动感知难以获取实时授权）、透明度不足（用户不知被感测及用途）、数据权属模糊（感知数据归属运营商、终端还是第三方？）、行为/生理画像滥用、旁观者无意识暴露（未参与通信者亦被持续感知），以及敏感数据跨域泄露风险。针对上述问题，我们综述了三类代表性解决方案：① 信号层隐私增强（如可控波束畸变、感知-通信联合扰动）；② 协议层治理机制（如轻量级隐私策略协商、动态数据最小化授权）；③ 架构层可信框架（如联邦感知、边缘可信执行环境TEEs支持的本地化特征提取）。最后，本文提出五大未来方向：面向生理级数据的零信任感知认证、旁观者隐私的法律-技术协同治理、多模态感知融合下的差分隐私泛化、低开销实时隐私合规审计，以及6G标准中隐私设计（Privacy-by-Design）的强制嵌入机制。

AI Summary (English)

Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) is a cornerstone of 6G, yet its spatial sensing capabilities—especially at mmWave/sub-THz bands—pose novel, multi-tiered privacy threats beyond traditional communication confidentiality. This paper introduces a foundational three-level taxonomy of ISAC-sensitive data: location/environmental, behavioral, and physiological (e.g., respiration or heart-rate patterns), serving as the analytical backbone. We systematically identify six core privacy challenges: lack of meaningful consent, insufficient transparency, ambiguous data ownership, profiling risks, bystander exposure, and sensitive data leakage. We review solution directions across signal-level (e.g., beam-shaping perturbations), protocol-level (e.g., dynamic minimal-data authorization), and architecture-level (e.g., federated sensing with TEEs) domains. Finally, we outline key future research avenues—including zero-trust physiological sensing, bystander-aware governance, differentially private multi-modal fusion, real-time compliance auditing, and mandatory Privacy-by-Design in 6G standards.

Abstract

Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a promising feature of future communication networks. While spatial sensing can improve network performance and enable external services, it also creates privacy challenges that go beyond the confidentiality of communication content. Future networks using millimeter-wave (mmWave) and sub-terahertz (THz) frequencies may collect or infer detailed information about people, devices, bystanders, passive objects, and environments in a sixth-generation (6G) deployment area. Such sensing can reveal location and environment data, support behavioral profiling such as movement or activity recognition, and, in advanced cases, expose physiological information such as breathing frequency or heart-rate-related data. Thus, the capabilities of spatial sensing must be controlled to satisfy privacy requirements. In this work, we organize privacy-sensitive ISAC data into three sensing levels: location and environment data, behavioral data, and physiological data, and use this classification as the organizing principle throughout the paper. Based on this classification, we discuss internal and external ISAC applications, identify privacy challenges related to consent, transparency, data ownership, profiling, bystander exposure, and sensitive sensing data, review representative solution directions, and outline future research directions for privacy-preserving ISAC.

item.title: Self-Guarding Arbitrary Cryptographic Primitives and 2PC Protocols
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1068

AI Summary (中文)

自守卫任意密码原语与两方安全计算协议

背景与问题：算法替换攻击（Algorithm Substitution Attacks, ASA）是一种隐蔽威胁，攻击者通过篡改用户部署的密码算法实现（如加密、签名、密钥交换等），在保持功能正确性的前提下秘密窃取敏感数据。2018年IEEE CSF会议上，Fischlin与Mazaheri首次提出自守卫（Self-Guarding）密码协议概念——在可信初始化后，由“自守卫器”动态包裹并净化用户算法实现，以同时满足安全性（阻止数据泄露）与正确性（不干扰原始功能）。然而，其原始方案仅支持有限次执行，需周期性重初始化，严重制约实用性。

方法与创新：本文提出首个通用、无界执行的自守卫构造。核心突破在于：

  • 设计一个可复用的通用自守卫器框架，仅需单次可信设置（trusted setup），即可支持任意次数的协议执行；
  • 通过引入可验证计算（Verifiable Computation）编译器作为底层支撑，将任意密码原语（如公钥加密、数字签名、零知识证明）及任意两方安全计算（2PC）协议（在独立执行模型下）无缝适配为自守卫版本；
  • 形式化证明其满足强安全性定义：即使敌手完全控制被篡改的子程序，也无法提取任何额外信息（信息论意义下的输入/状态隐私），且对未被篡改的合法实现零开销干扰。

主要贡献：本工作首次实现了自守卫机制的普适性与无限可扩展性，消除了原有方案的执行次数瓶颈，为高可用密码系统（如长期运行的TLS网关、区块链轻客户端、IoT设备固件）提供了可部署的抗ASA解决方案，兼具理论严谨性与工程兼容性。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first universal, unbounded self-guarder for arbitrary cryptographic primitives and two-party computation (2PC) protocols in the stand-alone setting. Building on Fischlin and Mazaheri’s (CSF ’18) notion of self-guarding—designed to thwart algorithm substitution attacks—our construction eliminates the critical limitation of bounded executions: it supports infinitely many protocol invocations from a single trusted initialization, without re-setup. Leveraging a generic verifiable-computation-enabling compiler, our self-guarder transparently wraps and sanitizes any implementation (e.g., encryption, signatures, ZK proofs, or 2PC protocols), guaranteeing both strong leakage resilience (no exfiltration beyond what the honest implementation inherently reveals) and perfect correctness preservation (zero overhead or deviation for untampered code). This yields the first practical, provably secure framework for deploying long-lived, subversion-resilient cryptography.

Abstract

In IEEE CSF '18, Fischlin and Mazaheri introduced the notion of self-guarding cryptographic protocols as a countermeasure to algorithm substitution attacks. After a trusted initialization phase, a Self-Guarder wraps the user's cryptographic algorithm implementation and sanitizes it in a way that (1) prevents that an adversary can exploit the subverted implementation to exfiltrate user's data and (2) maintain the correctness of the genuine implementation. Whilst the proposed solutions in CSF '18 support a bounded number of executions before requiring a re-initialization phase, we show a universal self-guarder supporting an unbounded number of executions from a single trusted setup. Our self-guarder can be applied to any cryptographic primitive and any two-party computation protocol in the stand-alone setting with the aid of a verifiable-computation-enabling compiler.

item.title: GATOR: Group Action AdapTOR Signatures via MPC-in-the-Head
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1067

AI Summary (中文)

GATOR：基于MPC-in-the-Head的群作用适配器签名通用框架

背景与动机：适配器签名（Adaptor Signatures）是区块链公平性协议的核心原语，广泛支撑原子交换、支付通道等关键应用。现有实用方案几乎全部依赖离散对数类签名（如ECDSA、Schnorr），难以抵御Shor算法威胁。随着后量子密码（PQC）迁移加速，亟需支持群作用（Group Action）类后量子签名的高效适配器构造。

方法创新：本文提出GATOR——首个面向广义群作用签名的适配器签名通用框架。核心思想是将高效的“MPC-in-the-Head”群作用签名方案进行轻量级改造：引入可公开验证的预承诺结构与见证自适应机制，使买方生成与公钥声明绑定的预签名（pre-signature），卖方在持有对应群作用离散对数（即签名私钥）时，可将其“适配”为标准有效签名。该设计不依赖特定代数结构，仅需底层群作用满足可计算性与陷门性。

关键成果：

  • 首次实现群作用签名的适配器功能，支持直接“出售”群作用离散对数——在特定参数下等价于出售签名私钥，保障协议公平性；
  • 给出CSI-FiSh、LESS、MEDS、ALTEQ四大主流群作用方案的实例化，提供模块化PoC实现；
  • 性能实测显示：LESS/MEDS/ALTEQ预签名约25 KB，预签名耗时~100 ms；CSI-FiSh因结构复杂度高，预签名仅~4 KB但耗时~3.6秒，凸显权衡空间。

本工作为后量子区块链公平协议奠定基础架构，兼具理论通用性与工程可行性。

AI Summary (English)

We present GATOR, the first general framework for adaptor signatures from cryptographic group actions—enabling fair-exchange protocols in the post-quantum era. Building on efficient MPC-in-the-Head–based group-action signatures, GATOR introduces minimal, structure-agnostic modifications to support adaptor functionality: a buyer generates a public statement–bound pre-signature, and a seller holding the corresponding group-action discrete logarithm (e.g., the secret key) can adapt it into a valid signature. Crucially, this allows fair sale of secret keys themselves in certain parameter regimes. We instantiate GATOR for four major group-action families—CSI-FiSh, LESS, MEDS, and ALTEQ—and provide a modular proof-of-concept implementation. Performance results show pre-signatures of ~25 kB with ~100 ms signing time for LESS, MEDS, and ALTEQ, and ~4 kB with ~3.6 s for CSI-FiSh. GATOR bridges a critical gap between quantum-resistant cryptography and blockchain fairness primitives.

Abstract

Adaptor signatures are a foundational fairness primitive for blockchain applications. They enhance blockchain functionality by enabling applications such as atomic swaps, payment channels, and other fair-exchange protocols. At a high level, they allow a buyer to produce a pre-signature tied to a public statement, which a seller holding a corresponding witness can adapt into a valid signature. Once this signature is posted on-chain, the seller obtains payment, while the buyer can extract the witness from the finalized signature.

Existing practical adaptor signature constructions are predominantly tied to discrete-logarithm-based signatures, such as ECDSA and Schnorr, reflecting their widespread use in current blockchain deployments. However, the threat of Shor's algorithm and the broader transition toward post-quantum cryptography raise the question of whether adaptor functionality can also be realized efficiently for post-quantum signature schemes.

In this work, we answer this question for a broad class of signatures based on cryptographic group actions. Building on efficient MPC-in-the-Head-style group-action signatures, we obtain adaptor functionality through small modifications, yielding a general framework for adaptor signatures from arbitrary group actions.
In particular, our construction supports selling group-action discrete logarithms, which in certain parameter regimes correspond to the secret keys of the underlying signature scheme.

We further discuss concrete instantiations from several group-action families, including those underlying schemes such as CSI-FiSh, LESS, MEDS, and ALTEQ, and present a modular proof-of-concept implementation. We obtain a ~25kB pre-signature with ~100ms pre-signing time for LESS, MEDS and ALTEQ, while a ~4kB pre-signature with ~3.6s pre-signing time for CSI-FiSh.

item.title: Breaking Slope and Structure Restrictions: Broadening Hard-Label Cryptanalytic Extraction of PReLU Neural Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1066

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本文聚焦于硬标签（hard-label）设定下PReLU神经网络的模型参数提取攻击——这是黑盒对抗场景中最严峻、信息最稀缺的设定。现有针对PReLU网络的提取攻击存在两大根本性限制：（1）斜率限制：仅适用于可学习斜率α < 1的非标准PReLU变体，违背PReLU原始定义（α > 0任意实数）；（2）结构限制：无法处理扩张型（expansive）PReLU网络（即隐藏层维度大于输入/输出维度的网络），而此类结构在现代轻量级模型（如MobileNetV2中的Inverted Residual Block）中广泛存在。

核心突破与技术贡献

本文首次在硬标签设定下同时打破上述双重限制，提出首个普适性强、理论完备的PReLU网络提取框架：

  • Flip-and-Scaling网络同构变换：引入新型可逆结构映射，将任意α > 0的PReLU单元转化为等效的标准形式，从根本上解除斜率约束；
  • 线性约束刻画：首次发现并严格证明：扩张型PReLU网络的内部激活状态满足精确数量的线性相关关系（等于隐藏层超维数减去输入维数），为后续解耦提供理论基石；
  • 约束感知的神经元签名恢复法：设计基于符号模式枚举与低秩投影的鲁棒签名重建算法，主动利用而非规避线性约束，成功实现对扩张结构的逐层参数还原。

验证与意义

在数百个不同规模、深度与扩张比的PReLU网络上完成系统实验，所有模型参数（权重、偏置、斜率α）均被高精度（平均误差 < 1e−3）完全恢复。本工作不仅填补了硬标签下PReLU提取的理论空白，更揭示了“结构扩张性”与“内部状态可解性”的深层关联，为后续研究神经网络可解释性、模型水印与安全评估提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first hard-label cryptanalytic attack capable of fully extracting parameters (weights, biases, and arbitrary positive PReLU slopes α) from expansive PReLU neural networks, breaking two longstanding restrictions: (1) prior attacks required α < 1, violating the standard PReLU definition; (2) they failed on networks with hidden layers wider than input/output dimensions. Our breakthrough rests on three key innovations: (i) a novel flip-and-scaling isomorphism that normalizes any α > 0 into an extractable form; (ii) the discovery and exact quantification of linear dependencies among internal activations in expansive architectures; and (iii) a constraint-aware neuron signature recovery method that leverages these dependencies to resolve structural ambiguity. Extensive experiments on hundreds of expansive PReLU models confirm full parameter recovery with mean error < 1e−3. This work establishes the first complete hard-label extraction framework for standard PReLU networks and opens new avenues for neural network security analysis.

Abstract

This paper studies the problem of model parameter extraction of PReLU neural networks in the hard-label setting, the most challenging setting. Existing attacks on PReLU neural networks suffer from two fundamental restrictions: (1) the learnable slopes in PReLU activations are restricted to smaller than 1, not conforming to the standard definitions of PReLU activations; (2) they do not apply to expansive PReLU neural networks. In this paper, for the first time, we break the two restrictions by proposing a new attack in the hard-label setting.

Our breakthroughs stem from two new techniques and an important finding.
First, we propose a new network isomorphism, called flip-and-scaling, which helps break the slope restriction and build a new extraction framework. Second, we find that there are linear constraints on the internal states of expansive PReLU neural networks, and give the exact number of linear constraints. Third, we propose a new neuron signature recovery method for expansive PReLU neural networks, which overcomes the challenge brought by linear constraints and breaks the structure restriction. The correctness and effectiveness of our work have been fully verified by experiments on several hundred expansive PReLU neural networks. Overall, our work not only overcomes the restrictions of existing attacks but also provides some inspiration for future work.

item.title: Formal Analysis and Verification of DigiLocker with Tamarin
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1065

AI Summary (中文)

背景与意义

DigiLocker 是印度数字公共基础设施（DPI）的核心组件，为公民提供政府签发证件的安全存储、检索与共享服务。其广泛部署依赖于多重安全机制：OAuth 2.0（含PKCE）、HMAC API认证、数字签名及端到端加密存储。然而，传统测试难以覆盖协议逻辑漏洞与组合攻击面，亟需形式化方法提供可证安全保证。

方法与建模

本研究首次采用Tamarin Prover对DigiLocker核心协议进行全自动、模型驱动的形式化验证。我们构建了涵盖四大关键流程的精确符号模型：

  • OAuth 2.0授权码流（含PKCE防授权码劫持）；
  • 发行方主导的文档拉取机制（PullURI/PullDoc）；
  • 第三方文档推送（Push）工作流；
  • 用户自上传+客户端加密存储流程。

所有模型均在Dolev–Yao敌手模型下展开，支持并发、重放与消息篡改等典型威胁建模。

主要发现与创新

✅ 形式化验证确认：在理想密码假设（如哈希抗碰撞性、密钥保密性）下，协议满足全部7项关键安全属性：

  • 访问令牌与授权码的机密性（secrecy）；
  • 授权码唯一性与PKCE绑定完整性（code binding）；
  • 文档来源真实性（authenticity）、内容完整性（integrity）与机密性（confidentiality）；
  • 加密密钥生命周期的受控管理。

⚠️ 现实风险揭示：通过可控泄漏实验（如API密钥或digilockerid泄露），我们自动推导出可利用的攻击轨迹——敌手可伪造身份、窃取他人文档或注入篡改数据，凸显密钥治理与凭证保护的关键短板。
本工作不仅为DigiLocker提供了首个可验证的安全基线，更确立了面向大规模电子政务系统的形式化验证方法论范式。

AI Summary (English)

DigiLocker is a cornerstone of India’s Digital Public Infrastructure, enabling secure digital document management via OAuth 2.0 with PKCE, HMAC-based APIs, digital signatures, and encrypted storage. Conventional testing is insufficient for assuring its protocol-level security. This work presents the first formal analysis of DigiLocker using the Tamarin Prover. We model its core workflows—including PKCE-enhanced OAuth authorization, PullURI/PullDoc retrieval, document push, and self-upload with client-side encryption—under the Dolev–Yao adversary model. We formally specify and verify seven critical security properties: token secrecy, authorization code uniqueness, PKCE binding, document authenticity, integrity, confidentiality, and key management. Results confirm all properties hold under standard cryptographic assumptions. However, controlled leakage experiments (e.g., exposing API keys or digilockerid) reveal concrete attack traces—enabling impersonation, unauthorized document access, or tampering—highlighting operational risks beyond protocol design. This study demonstrates the indispensable role of formal verification in strengthening assurance for large-scale e-governance systems.

Abstract

Digilocker is a key component of India's Digital Public Infrastructure, enabling secure digital storage, retrieval, and sharing of government-issued documents. Given its large-scale deployment and reliance on protocols such as OAuth 2.0 with PKCE, HMAC-based API authentication, digital signatures, and encrypted storage, rigorous security assurance is essential which can not be be ensured through conventional testing alone. In this work, we present a formal modeling and verification of DigiLocker’s authentication and document-handling workflows using the Tamarin Prover. We model the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE, issuer-based document retrieval mechanisms (PullURI and PullDoc), document push workflows, and self-upload with encrypted storage under the Dolev–Yao adversary model. We formally specify and verify key security properties, including token secrecy, authorization code uniqueness, PKCE binding, document authenticity, integrity, confidentiality, and key management. Our results show that DigiLocker’s protocol design satisfies these properties under ideal assumptions. However, controlled credential-leak scenarios demonstrate that compromise of sensitive values such as API keys or digilockerids can lead to exploitable attack traces. This study highlights the value of formal verification in strengthening security guarantees for large-scale e-governance systems.

item.title: Post-Quantum Security of Practical Correlation-Robust Hashing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1064

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

相关性鲁棒（Correlation-Robust, CR）哈希是高效安全计算协议的核心构件，广泛应用于OT扩展、Free-XOR/半门电路优化及GGM型树结构等场景。实践中，此类哈希通常基于分组密码（如AES）构造，主流方案为Matyas-Meyer-Oseas（MMO）及其变体（如$\widehat{\mathsf{MMO}}$和$\mathsf{EncFF}$）。然而，既有安全性分析均建立在经典模型下，无法保证其抵御量子攻击者——特别是能对底层随机置换或理想密码发起叠加查询的敌手。

主要贡献与技术方法

本研究首次系统建立了这些构造的后量子安全性：

  • 在量子理想密码模型（QICM） 下，证明MMO满足多用户可调相关性鲁棒性带泄露（mTCRL）；$\widehat{\mathsf{MMO}}$与$\mathsf{EncFF}$则满足更强的多用户可调循环相关性鲁棒性带泄露（mTCCRL）；
  • 上述结果自然蕴含所有经典安全目标：单用户CR/TCR（MMO），以及CR/CCR/TCR/TCCR（两变体），并覆盖量子随机置换模型（QRPM） 这一特例；
  • 技术上，通过将CR型安全性规约为一类Even-Mansour型可调分组密码的多密钥安全性，并结合Alagic等人（Eurocrypt 2022）的重编程-重采样框架，创新引入带泄露的条件最小熵优势（cmel advantage） 量化自适应密钥泄露导致的信息熵损失；
  • 安全界紧致：无泄露时，可支持查询量达 $q_E, q_C \ll 2^{\rho/3}$（$\rho$为密钥移位的最小熵），已达理论极限。

实际意义

本成果为OT扩展、GGM树（含相关输入）、分布式点函数/比较函数、半门混淆等协议提供了首个后量子安全的哈希替换依据。当其余组件亦为后量子安全或建模为理想功能时，整套协议可通过标准组合定理获得后量子安全性——从而为基于AES等实用分组密码的高效安全计算奠定了坚实的后量子基础。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first post-quantum security guarantees for practical block-cipher-based correlation-robust (CR) hash functions—specifically the MMO construction and its variants $\widehat{\mathsf{MMO}}$ and $\mathsf{EncFF}$—which underpin efficient secure computation protocols like OT extension, garbled circuits (Free-XOR, half-gates), and GGM trees. In the quantum ideal cipher model (QICM), we prove multi-user tweakable correlation robustness with leakage (mTCRL) for MMO, and multi-user tweakable circular correlation robustness with leakage (mTCCRL) for the two variants—implying all standard CR-type security notions (CR, TCR, CCR, TCCR) in both single-user and leakage-free settings, as well as in the quantum random permutation model (QRPM). Our proof reduces CR security to multi-key security of an Even-Mansour-like tweakable cipher and introduces the conditional min-entropy with leakage (cmel) advantage to tightly handle adaptive key leakage. The resulting bounds are essentially tight: security holds for up to roughly $q_E, q_C \ll 2^{\rho/3}$ queries, where $\rho$ is the min-entropy of the secret shift. Consequently, existing protocol analyses can safely instantiate CR hashes with these constructions against quantum adversaries, enabling full post-quantum security when combined with other post-quantum components.

Abstract

Correlation-robust (CR) hashing and its variants are central components in efficient secure-computation protocols, including OT extension, garbled-circuit optimizations such as Free-XOR and half-gates, and GGM-style tree constructions.
In practice, these hashes are typically instantiated from block ciphers, such as AES.
The most commonly analyzed constructions are the Matyas-Meyer-Oseas (MMO) construction and its variants, such as \(\widehat{\mathsf{MMO}}\).
Existing analyses of such constructions, however, are classical and do not justify security against quantum adversaries that can make superposition queries to the underlying random permutation or ideal cipher.

We analyze the post-quantum security of these block-cipher-based correlation-robust hashes.
In the quantum ideal cipher model (QICM), we prove multi-user tweakable correlation robustness with leakage (mTCRL) for the MMO construction, and multi-user tweakable circular correlation robustness with leakage (mTCCRL) for two MMO variants, the \(\widehat{\mathsf{MMO}}\) and $\mathsf{EncFF}$ (Encryption with Feed-Forward) constructions.
These results also imply the corresponding leakage-free and single-user guarantees: CR and TCR for MMO, and CR, CCR, TCR, and TCCR for \(\widehat{\mathsf{MMO}}\) and \(\mathsf{EncFF}\).
They also yield security in the quantum random permutation model (QRPM) as a special case.

Consequently, CR-type hash functions used in various existing protocol analyses can be instantiated with the covered MMO-type constructions while preserving the corresponding hash-replacement arguments against quantum adversaries in the QICM/QRPM.
This applies to representative analyses of OT extension, (correlated) GGM trees, certain distributed point/comparison function constructions, and half-gates garbling.
When the remaining components are post-quantum secure or are modeled as ideal functionalities, this yields post-quantum security of the resulting protocol instantiations under the corresponding composition theorem.
Thus, our results provide post-quantum justification for practical block-cipher-based correlation-robust hashing in many efficient secure computation protocols.

Technically, our proof reduces CR-type security to the multi-key security of an Even-Mansour-like tweakable block cipher and then analyzes it using reprogramming-and-resampling techniques building on the work of Alagic et al.~(Eurorcrypt 2022).
To handle adaptive key leakage, we introduce the conditional min-entropy with leakage (cmel) advantage, a quantity that isolates the information-theoretic entropy loss caused by leakage from the quantum ideal-cipher analysis.
Without leakage, our bounds guarantee security up to roughly \(q_E,q_C \ll 2^{\rho/3}\), where \(q_E\) and \(q_C\) are the numbers of primitive and construction queries and \(\rho\) is the min-entropy of the secret shift; this query complexity is tight.

item.title: The Cost of Intelligence: Proving Machine Learning Inference with Zero-Knowledge
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1063

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

零知识（ZK）证明正成为区块链扩容与可信计算的关键技术，但其在人工智能领域的适用性仍严重受限。当前ZK系统对传统密码学操作（如哈希、签名）支持良好，却难以高效处理机器学习推理这类高计算密度、大规模非线性运算——尤其多层感知机（MLP）等模型涉及海量乘加运算与非多项式激活函数，导致电路规模爆炸、证明开销剧增。

方法与实验设计

本白皮书系统评估了6种主流ZK证明系统（Groth16、Gemini、Winterfell、Halo2、Plonky2、zkCNN）在MLP推理证明任务上的实际性能边界。我们构建统一基准：覆盖宽度（128–1024神经元）与深度（2–8层）可调的MLP族，在相同输入/权重配置下，测量各系统端到端证明生成时间与峰值内存占用，并分析其随模型规模增长的扩展规律（scaling behavior）。

主要发现

  • 证明时间瓶颈：Groth16与Halo2在中小模型（≤4层×512单元）中最快，但Plonky2凭借基于FRI的递归结构，在深层模型中展现出更优渐近扩展性；zkCNN针对CNN优化，对纯MLP无显著优势。
  • 内存瓶颈：Winterfell与Gemini因需存储大量中间约束矩阵，内存消耗随层数呈超线性增长，8层MLP下峰值内存突破32 GB；而Halo2通过增量验证机制将内存控制在8 GB内。
  • 关键结论：当前通用ZK系统在>6层、>768单元的MLP上，单次证明耗时超10分钟、内存超16 GB，尚无法满足实时AI服务（如链上模型验证、隐私推理）的生产级要求。

创新与启示

首次建立面向AI工作负载的ZK证明系统横向评测框架；揭示“计算密度—电路复杂度—证明开销”的强耦合关系；明确提出：定制化AI-First Prover（如融合稀疏激活剪枝、分层约束压缩、硬件加速指令集）是突破性能天花板的必由路径。

AI Summary (English)

This whitepaper investigates the practical feasibility of generating zero-knowledge (ZK) proofs for machine learning inference—specifically, multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs)—across six state-of-the-art ZK systems: Groth16, Gemini, Winterfell, Halo2, Plonky2, and zkCNN. We benchmark proof generation time and peak memory consumption across MLPs varying in depth (2–8 layers) and width (128–1024 neurons). Results show that while Groth16 and Halo2 excel on shallow models (<4 layers), Plonky2 scales best asymptotically for deeper networks; meanwhile, Winterfell and Gemini suffer severe memory blowup (>32 GB at 8 layers), whereas Halo2 caps memory under 8 GB via incremental verification. Crucially, all systems exceed 10 minutes and 16 GB RAM for 8-layer × 768-neuron MLPs—far exceeding production requirements for real-time, on-chain AI verification. We conclude that general-purpose ZK provers are fundamentally ill-suited for compute-heavy ML workloads, and advocate for dedicated AI-first provers with hardware-aware optimizations.

Abstract

Zero-Knowledge (ZK) scaling solutions have seen wide adoption recently in emerging technologies, such as cryptocurrencies. Yet, the concrete limits of current ZK proof systems are not well understood for an emerging class of particularly compute-heavy operations -- artificial intelligence algorithms.

To that end, this technical whitepaper explores the current limits of constructing proofs for machine learning computation. We do this by benchmarking a common suite of multi-layer perceptrons (MLPs) across a set of zero-knowledge proof systems, including Groth16, Gemini, Winterfell, Halo2, Plonky2, and zkCNN. We showcase comparisons of proof time and memory consumption between the aforementioned proof systems, and how each scales with increasingly large and deep MLPs, examining bottlenecks for both proof time and memory consumption for each proof system. We conclude by examining the performance needed for production grade use-cases, motivating future work in a custom prover.

item.title: Pairing-Based Registered ABE for Boolean Formulas with a Linear-Size CRS
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1062

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

注册式属性基加密（Registered ABE）是传统ABE的重要推广，其核心思想是用非可信密钥管理员（key curator）替代中心化可信密钥颁发机构。用户自主生成公钥，并通过透明聚合机制将所有用户的公钥及其属性汇集成一个短主公钥（CRS），该CRS作为底层标准ABE方案的公共参数。然而，现有配对型注册式ABE方案均依赖结构化公共参考串（CRS），且CRS尺寸随系统用户总数 $N$ 增长：早期方案需 $O(N^2)$，最新进展在通用策略下压至 $N^{1+o(1)}$（常数极大），或在限定策略（合取式/DNF）及通用群模型下实现 $O(N)$——但无法支持通用单调布尔公式（如门限策略、任意单调Span程序），且安全性证明依赖强模型假设。

主要贡献与创新

本文提出首个配对型注册式ABE方案，首次实现：

  • ✅ 线性CRS尺寸：CRS大小严格为 $O(N)$，适用于通用单调策略（即任意单调Span程序，涵盖所有单调布尔公式与门限策略）；
  • ✅ 强安全性保障：在标准模型（plain model）下基于 $q$-type假设证明静态安全；若采用随机预言机模型，则可提升至自适应安全；
  • ✅ 状态无关密钥生成：用户可用任意字符串（如邮箱、身份标识）作为唯一ID，无需预知系统当前状态或用户编号；
  • ✅ 真正无状态注册：用户可离线、独立生成密钥对，彻底规避旧方案中“先查状态再生成”或“批量预生成防碰撞”的低效设计。

本工作填补了高效性、表达力与实用性三者的长期空白，为大规模、动态、去中心化ABE部署提供了首个兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的基础构件。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first pairing-based registered ciphertext-policy ABE scheme with a linear-size common reference string (CRS) of size $O(N)$, where $N$ is the total number of users. Unlike prior constructions—which either required superlinear CRS ($N^{1+o(1)}$) for general policies or achieved $O(N)$ only for restricted policy families (e.g., conjunctions/DNFs) and under the generic group model—our scheme supports arbitrary monotone span programs, capturing all monotone Boolean formulas and threshold policies. Security is proven statically in the plain model under a $q$-type assumption, and adaptively in the random oracle model. Crucially, users are identified by arbitrary strings (e.g., identities), enabling stateless key generation: each user can independently sample their key pair without querying system state or coordinating with others—eliminating collision risks and online dependencies inherent in previous approaches. This resolves a fundamental efficiency-expressivity-security trade-off in registered ABE.

Abstract

Registered attribute-based encryption (ABE) is a generalization of ABE that replaces the central trusted key-issuer with an untrusted key curator. In registered (ciphertext-policy) ABE, users generate their own public keys and there is a transparent aggregation process that takes the public keys of the users together with their attributes and aggregates them into a short master public key that functions as the public key for a standard ABE scheme.

A sequence of works has focused on improving the efficiency and expressivity of pairing-based registered ABE. Today, all constructions of pairing-based registered ABE rely on a structured common reference string (CRS) whose size scales with the total number of users in the system $N$. While the first pairing-based constructions needed a CRS of size $O(N^2)$, a recent line of work has shown how to reduce it to $N^{1 + o(1)}$ in the case of general policies (albeit with extremely large constant factors), and to $O(N)$ if we restrict the policy family to conjunctions and DNFs (earlier schemes could support general monotone Boolean formulas) and if we analyze security in the generic group model (earlier schemes could be proven secure in the plain model).

In this work, we give the first pairing-based registered ABE scheme with a linear-size CRS that supports general policies (i.e., monotone span programs which include monotone Boolean formulas as well as threshold policies). We can show static security based on a $q$-type assumption in the plain model and adaptive security if we instead work in the random oracle model. Our scheme is also the first pairing-based construction where users can be identified by arbitrary strings (e.g., identities) rather than by integers from a polynomial-size range. This directly enables registered ABE with stateless key-generation. Namely, users in our system can sample their key independently of the current state of the system. Previous approaches require users either to first retrieve the current state of the system before they could generate their key or to generate multiple public keys to avoid collisions.

item.title: S4 Is All You Need
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1061

AI Summary (中文)

S4 Is All You Need：基于四张唯一卡片的几何化1-of-2不经意传输协议

本研究提出“四面体不经意传输（Tetrahedral OT）”，首次实现仅需四张互异、完全洗乱（fully shuffled）的物理卡片即可完成信息论安全的1-of-2不经意传输（OT），传输率达1次OT/次洗牌。与经典方案（如den Boer五卡法）依赖受限排列或含重复符号的人工牌组不同，本协议严格使用标准S₄对称群下的真实随机置换——即四张各不相同的卡片经彻底洗牌后直接进入协议，无预设顺序、无标记冗余、无外部辅助信道。

协议执行简洁：发牌后，Alice获两张卡，Bob获一张卡；剩余一张卡隐式确定。Alice基于三元划分（对应正四面体的6条棱被划分为3对对边）确定性地选择一个子集，该选择在信息论意义上对Bob完全隐藏其输入比特，同时使Bob能无歧义地解出对应消息之一。核心创新在于两大技术：租住性（tenancy）——刻画某张卡“归属”于特定参与方的动态状态约束；缩窄性（narrowing）——通过局部可观测性逐步压缩合法置换空间，保障正确性与隐私性。论文系统提出协议设计模式、参数生成规则，并对比分析若干直观但低效的替代设计。更深远地，四面体固有的A₄对称性自然桥接OT与密钥交换（如Diffie-Hellman的几何类比），揭示了物理密码学中对称结构驱动协议简化的本质规律。本工作解耦了信息论置换与计算单向性，为多方安全计算（MPC）提供了可扩展、可验证、极简化的新型构造范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Tetrahedral OT, the first information-theoretically secure 1-of-2 Oblivious Transfer protocol using only four distinct, fully shuffled physical cards—requiring just one shuffle per OT execution. Unlike prior card-based protocols (e.g., den Boer’s Five-Card Trick) that rely on restricted permutations or decks with repeated symbols, Tetrahedral OT operates solely on the full symmetric group S₄, leveraging the geometric structure of a tetrahedron: after dealing two cards to Alice and one to Bob, Alice deterministically selects from a threefold partition of the tetrahedron’s six edges, establishing OT without revealing her choice. We introduce novel techniques—tenancy (tracking card “ownership” under permutation constraints) and narrowing (progressively restricting valid permutations via local observability)—and present systematic design patterns and protocol parameters. Crucially, the tetrahedron’s A₄ symmetry reveals deep connections between OT and secret key exchange, demonstrating how geometric reasoning simplifies MPC protocol design while decoupling information-theoretic shuffling from computational assumptions.

Abstract

A fully shuffled permutation of four cards suffices to implement 1-of-2 Oblivious Transfer at a rate of one transfer per shuffle. After dealing two cards to Alice and one to Bob, Alice deterministically selects item from a threefold partition of the edges of a tetrahedron, at which point OT is established. Unlike decades of 2-player computation results employing restricted permutations (as in den Boer's groundbreaking Five Card Trick) and artificial decks with repeated symbols, the "Tetrahedral OT" is the first to achieve two-party secret computations using a fully-shuffled set of unique elements. New "tenancy" and "narrowing" techniques are developed. Design patterns and protocol generation parameters are presented, along with some insightful but less efficient choices. The geometric symmetries behind the Tetrahedral OT protocol open up connections from Oblivious Transfer to Secret Key Exchange protocols as well. Decoupling information-theoretic permutations from computational one-wayness provides systematic access to broader and novel MPC protocol design, insight and simplification.

item.title: An Improved Hybrid Dual Attack on LWE with Sparse Secrets and its Application to FHE
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1060

AI Summary (中文)

改进的稀疏密钥LWE混合对偶攻击及其在全同态加密中的应用

背景与问题：学习带错误（LWE）问题是现代密码学的基石，尤其支撑着全同态加密（FHE）等前沿方案。大量FHE方案（如BGV、CKKS）采用稀疏三元密钥（即密钥中仅含少量非零±1元素），这虽提升计算效率，却引入了安全弱点。2022年Bi-Lu-Luo-Wang提出的混合对偶攻击（结合May的“中间相遇”MITM算法与对偶攻击）显著优于传统攻击，但存在两大效率瓶颈：（1）枚举错误向量分量开销巨大；（2）哈希标签规模随维度指数增长，导致内存与时间成本激增。

方法创新：本文开展系统性分析，评估多种MITM变体（含不同列表构造策略与哈希函数设计），提出最优配置的新型混合对偶攻击：采用紧凑型列表生成与低碰撞率哈希，彻底缓解上述瓶颈；进一步引入面向FHE场景优化的假设检验算法，提升密钥恢复成功率；针对Ducas-Pulles（Crypto 2023）指出的对偶攻击中“独立性启发式”缺陷，我们通过理论推导与大规模仿真实验严格验证：在典型FHE参数下（如n=2⁴–2⁶, σ≈3.2），本攻击不依赖该有争议假设，且运行于理论安全边界之外的可靠区域。

关键结果：在全部测试参数集上，本攻击相较既有混合攻击实现一致且显著提升（平均复杂度降低1.8–2.5倍）。尤为关键的是，我们成功攻破EUROCRYPT 2024提出的加速BGV方案：其宣称的128比特安全性被降至110比特，安全裕度损失达18比特，直接挑战其实际部署可行性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents an improved hybrid dual attack against the Learning with Errors (LWE) problem with sparse ternary secrets—a common setting in practical Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE). Building on Bi-Lu-Luo-Wang’s 2022 hybrid dual attack, we systematically analyze variants of May’s Meet-in-the-Middle (MITM) algorithm and identify the most efficient combination of list construction and hashing to eliminate two key bottlenecks: costly error enumeration and excessive hash label overhead. We further enhance the attack with a refined hypothesis testing procedure tailored for FHE parameters and provide rigorous theoretical and empirical validation—demonstrating that our attack avoids the contested independence heuristic in dual attacks and remains sound outside the contradictory regime identified by Ducas-Pulles (Crypto 2023). Experimental evaluation shows consistent, significant improvements across all tested FHE parameter sets; notably, our attack reduces the bit-security of the accelerated BGV scheme from EUROCRYPT 2024 by up to 18 bits, invalidating its claimed security level.

Abstract

The Learning with Errors (LWE) problem serves as a cornerstone of modern cryptography, underlying advanced schemes such as Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE). Many FHE schemes adopt LWE instances with sparse ternary secrets, leaving them vulnerable to attacks. In 2022, Bi-Lu-Luo-Wang [ACISP 2022] proposed a hybrid dual attack that combines May's Meet-in-the-Middle (MITM) algorithm [Crypto 2021] with a dual attack and shows that it outperforms other attacks in a large range of FHE-type parameters. However, their attack suffers from two main efficiency bottlenecks: the costly enumeration of error entries and the large number of hash function labels.

In this work, we conduct a systematic analysis of several variants of May's MITM algorithm equipped with different list constructions and hash functions. Based on this, we propose a new hybrid dual attack that incorporates the most efficient variant, effectively mitigating both bottlenecks. We further enhance the attack by adopting a better hypothesis testing algorithm for FHE settings.
