Addressing recent concerns raised by Ducas-Pulles [Crypto 2023] regarding the independence heuristic in dual attacks, we provide a rigorous theoretical and
empirical analysis. We demonstrate that, for typical FHE parameters, our attack does not rely on the problematic independence heuristic and lies outside the contradictory regime. Finally, we compare our attack with previous hybrid attacks, showing consistent and significant improvements across all evaluated cases. In particular, our results invalidate the accelerated BGV scheme in [EUROCRYPT 2024] by reducing its bit-security below the claimed security level, with the most extreme case being 18 bits lower.

item.title: Threshold Traitor Tracing with Partial-Insider Resilience
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1059

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

阈值叛徒追踪（Threshold Traitor Tracing, TTT）是应对门限加密中合谋攻击的关键技术，旨在识别并追踪参与构造非法解密设备（即“解码器”）的恶意参与者。然而，Boneh 等人（Crypto’24）提出的原始定义未涵盖部分内鬼（partial insiders）场景：即敌手可在系统正常运行中获取由诚实方发布的部分解密结果（如Shamir门限解密中的单个份额）。在此设定下，非法解码器可依赖外部买家提供的额外部分解密输入；更严重的是，合谋者可利用观测到的部分解密信息主动规避追踪，甚至嫁祸于诚实方。

方法与创新

本文提出首个在强区分型解码器模型（distinguishing decoders）下具备部分内鬼弹性的TTT构造，显著超越Bormet等人（EPrint’26）的弱模型（仅支持全解密输出、仅允许对有效密文查询部分解密、且依赖可信中心生成密钥）。我们的方案：

  • 无需可信中心，实现完全去中心化的密钥分发；
  • 支持任意密文的部分解密预言机访问（包括无效/恶意构造密文），保障强安全性；
  • 提出一项通用转换技术：借助非交互式零知识证明（NIZK-PoK），将仅对有效密文的部分解密预言机下的可追踪性，安全提升至对任意密文的部分解密预言机下仍保持可追踪。该转换独立重要，为后续研究提供了简洁化分析路径——未来工作可聚焦于更易处理的有效密文设定，再通过本转换自动获得更强保证。

主要贡献

本工作首次实现了强模型下免信任、抗任意部分解密查询的阈值叛徒追踪，填补了理论空白，并为现实部署（如DRM、密钥托管系统）提供了更鲁棒的安全基石。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first threshold traitor tracing (TTT) scheme resilient against partial insiders in the strong distinguishing decoder model—overcoming key limitations of prior work. Unlike Bormet et al. (EPrint’26), our construction requires no trusted dealer, supports a partial decryption oracle on arbitrary ciphertexts (not just valid ones), and remains fully traceable even when colluders exploit observed partial decryptions to evade detection or frame honest parties. A core technical contribution is a generic transformation: using NIZK proofs of knowledge, we lift traceability from the “valid-ciphertext-only” partial decryption oracle setting to the significantly stronger “arbitrary-ciphertext” setting. This transform is of independent interest—it strengthens all prior TTT results relying on restricted oracles and enables future analyses to focus solely on the simpler valid-ciphertext case while inheriting full robustness.

Abstract

Threshold traitor tracing (Boneh et al. Crypto'24) addresses collusion in threshold encryption by tracing parties who collude to build illegal decryption devices called decoders.
However, the original definition does not capture settings where adversaries can access partial decryptions published during normal system operation.
In such settings, decoders sold to external buyers could depend on inputting additional partial decryptions from honest parties. Moreover, colluders may exploit observed partial decryptions when constructing a decoder to evade tracing, or even frame honest parties. Recently, Bormet et al. (EPrint'26) addressed this by introducing threshold traitor tracing in the presence of partial insiders. Their construction, however, works only in a weaker model, where decoders are assumed to output the full decryption, and colluders are assumed to observe only partial decryptions of valid ciphertexts. Additionally, they use a trusted dealer for key generation.

In this work, we present the first construction resilient against partial insiders in the stronger model of distinguishing decoders. Furthermore, our construction does not rely on a trusted dealer and remains traceable when colluders have access to a partial decryption oracle.

As part of this, we show how to generically lift traceability under a partial decryption oracle for valid ciphertexts to traceability under a partial decryption oracle for arbitrary ciphertexts using NIZK-PoKs. This transform is of independent interest, directly strengthening prior results that consider only valid-ciphertext partial decryption oracles, and allowing future analyses to focus on the simpler valid-ciphertext setting.

item.title: Efficient MPC-Based Modulus Conversion for Threshold FHE Decryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1058

AI Summary (中文)

高效基于MPC的模数转换技术及其在门限全同态加密解密中的应用

本文提出了一套无需比特分解的新型算术安全多方计算（MPC）模数转换协议，用于在不同模数间高效转换秘密共享值。给定源模数 $q$ 下的共享 $[x]_q$，协议可在满足 $|x| < \min(q,t)/2$ 的温和界约束下，输出目标模数 $t$ 下的等价共享 $[x]_t$。我们设计了三种互补变体：

  • 幂二模数专用协议：当 $q=2^a$、$t=2^b$ 时结构极简，仅需1次公开（opening）与常数级预处理；
  • 素数目标模协议：支持任意源模 $q$ 与素数目标模 $t$，通信与计算开销低；
  • 通用目标模协议：通过引入中间素数模实现对任意 $t$（含合数）的支持，仍保持常数轮次与小量预处理。

所有协议均在算术黑盒模型下构建，可无缝适配任意支持基本模运算的MPC底层（如BGW、SPDZ、Overdrive等）。

作为核心应用，我们将该技术应用于格基全同态加密（FHE）的门限解密，首次实现了对BFV、BGV等主流方案的高效、统计意义下通用可组合（UC）安全门限解密协议。新协议为专用MPC构造，仅需常数轮通信（≤3轮），且完全规避噪声泛洪（noise flooding）——这意味着FHE参数可按单方解密标准优化，无需预留额外噪声余量，显著提升效率与安全性。

实验对比表明性能飞跃：以BFV（密文模 $2^{64}$）为例，相较ACM CCS 2025最新工作（Zyskind等），我们的预处理相关随机性需求从约17,000比特降至仅63比特（降幅超99.6%），同时保持恶意敌手下的统计UC安全性。

AI Summary (English)

We propose novel modulus conversion protocols for arithmetic MPC that convert secret-shared values $[x]_q$ to $[x]_t$ without bit decomposition, under the mild bound $|x| < \min(q,t)/2$. Our three variants—optimized for power-of-two moduli, prime target moduli, and arbitrary targets via an intermediate prime—all require only a constant number of openings and negligible preprocessing (e.g., 63 bits for BFV with $q=2^{64}$), and are formulated in the arithmetic black-box model for broad MPC compatibility.

As the main application, we construct the first efficient, statistically UC-secure threshold decryption protocols for lattice-based FHE (BFV, BGV), achieving constant-round communication, no noise flooding, and optimal FHE parameter selection. Compared to the state-of-the-art (Zyskind et al., CCS 2025), our protocol reduces preprocessing correlated randomness by >99.6%—from ~17,000 bits to just 63—while guaranteeing malicious security.

Abstract

We present new techniques for converting secret-shared values between different moduli in arithmetic MPC, without relying on bit decomposition. More concretely, our protocols convert a sharing \([x]_q\) over a source modulus \(q\) into a sharing \([x]_t\) over a target modulus \(t\), under a mild bound on the size of \(x\). We give three variants: a particularly simple protocol for power-of-two moduli, a protocol for arbitrary source modulus and prime target modulus, and a general protocol for arbitrary target modulus via an intermediate prime modulus. All variants use only a constant number of openings and a small amount of preprocessing. We present them in the arithmetic black box model, so they can be instantiated on top of any MPC protocol supporting basic modular arithmetic.

As a main application, we use these techniques to construct efficient threshold decryption protocols for lattice-based fully homomorphic encryption (FHE), including BFV, BGV, and related schemes. The resulting protocols are special-purpose MPC protocols with a small constant number of rounds. They avoid noise flooding, allowing the parameters of the underlying FHE scheme to be chosen without making room for additional decryption noise.

The resulting protocols achieve statistical UC security against malicious adversaries.

We improve substantially on previous work on MPC-based threshold FHE decryption: as a concrete example, the state-of-the-art protocol by Zyskind et al. (ACM CCS 2025) implements decryption of the BFV scheme (with ciphertext modulus $2^{64}$), using about 17.000 bits of preprocessed correlated randomness, while we need only 63.

item.title: Computer-Aided Proof for Extended Generalized Feistel Networks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1057

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

多分支广义Feistel网络（GFN）是构造轻量级分组密码的核心结构，其安全性依赖于轮数设计。Berger等人（SAC 2013）提出的扩展广义Feistel网络（EGFN）以矩阵形式统一建模多种Feistel类结构，但针对任意矩阵参数，判定达到伪随机置换（PRP）和强伪随机置换（SPRP）安全所需的最小轮数极为困难——尤其当分支数增加时，传统手工证明需枚举海量碰撞路径，极易出错且不可扩展。

方法与工具

本文提出AutoEGFN：首个全自动、可验证的EGFN安全性分析工具。其核心是通过符号化计算三个关键参数 $r_1$、$r_2$、$r_3$（分别对应差分/线性传播深度、轮函数独立性阈值及结构对称性约束），自动推导PRP/SPRP安全所需的最小轮数。我们给出了完整的形式化安全性证明，严格保障工具输出的有效性（validity）与可靠性（soundness）。

主要成果与创新

  • 首次系统完成Type-1/Type-2 GFN在2–32分支下的全范围(S)PRP轮数分析（此前仅8/16分支有手工证明）；
  • 对YI11、DFLM19、DDGP22、Type-1.x、SH/TH、Nyberg、SM及BMT等9类主流EGFN变体提供首个无限制条件（即不假设置换层为随机置换）的精确(S)PRP轮数证明；
  • 工具运行高效：32分支结构的完整证明仅需数分钟，远超人工能力；
  • 成果直接支撑更安全、更高效的EGFN密码设计与标准化评估，显著提升实用密码工程的可信度与开发效率。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces AutoEGFN, the first computer-aided tool for automatically determining the minimal number of rounds required to achieve pseudorandom permutation (PRP) and strong PRP (SPRP) security for Extended Generalized Feistel Networks (EGFNs). AutoEGFN computes three structural parameters ($r_1$, $r_2$, $r_3$) via symbolic matrix analysis, and its soundness is formally proven. Applied to nine major EGFN variants—including Type-1/2, YI11, DFLM19, DDGP22, Type-1.x, SH/TH, Nyberg, SM, and BMT—it delivers the first unrestricted (S)PRP security proofs (i.e., without assuming ideal round permutations). Notably, it fully resolves (S)PRP bounds for Type-1/2 GFNs across 2 to 32 branches, whereas prior work only covered 8- and 16-branch BMT-EGFNs manually. All proofs complete in minutes, even for 32-branch cases—demonstrating scalability, reliability, and practical impact for cryptographic design and standardization.

Abstract

(Multi-branch) Generalized Feistel Network~(GFN) enables the construction of block ciphers from non-linear components with small domains, and has been adopted in various block ciphers. Berger et al. (SAC 2013) introduced the Extended Generalized Feistel Network~(EGFN), which unified and extended existing Feistel-like structures by using a matrix representation.

Given an arbitrary matrix, it is typically difficult to determine how many EGFN rounds are sufficient for pseudorandom permutation (PRP) and strong PRP (SPRP) security. Remarkably, security proofs for structures with a larger number of branches have to analyze a huge amount of collision events, which is overly complicated and prone to errors.

To remedy this situation, we present AutoEGFN, a computer-aided proof tool that determines the number of rounds sufficient for PRP and SPRP security for various variants of EGFN. The tool operates by calculating three parameters: $r_1$, $r_2$, and $r_3$. The validity and soundness of AutoEGFN are formally established by a detailed security proof. To demonstrate the effectiveness of AutoEGFN, we have applied it to multiple structures such as Type-1/2 GFN (Zheng et al., CRYPTO 1989), YI11's Type-1 GFN (Yanagihara and Iwata, CANS 2011), DFLM19's GFN (Derbez et al., FSE 2019), DDGP22's GFN (Delaune et al., INDOCRYPT 2022), Type-1.x GFN (Yanagihara and Iwata, IEICE 2014), SH/TH GFN (Yanagihara and Iwata, CANS 2011), Nyberg's GFN (Nyberg, ASIACRYPT 1996), SM's GFN (Suzaki and Minematsu, FSE 2010), and BMT's EGFN (Berger et al., SAC 2013). As a result, we provide a systematic analysis of the (S)PRP security for Type-1 and Type-2 structures for different numbers of branches. Our tool efficiently determines the concrete number of rounds required to ensure PRP and SPRP security for EGFNs with different branch numbers. For comparison, previous work only proved the (S)PRP security for 8- and 16-branch BMT's EGFN. Our tool completes the proof within several minutes, even for variants with $32$ branches. Meanwhile, for the other structures, we provide the first concrete (S)PRP security proofs without any restrictions on their permutation layers. Furthermore, AutoEGFN will significantly contribute to the enhancement of EGFN designs and implementations in various cryptographic applications.

item.title: Multivariate Polynomial Inference in a Cryptographic Setting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1056

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

当前密码分析中，对递归构造的双变量多项式（如通过重复加法与乘法生成）的推断方法已取得显著进展。然而，现实密码方案（如全同态加密FHE中的噪声增长建模、多密钥聚合协议）常涉及多元（≥3变量）多项式结构，现有双变量技术难以直接扩展，存在理论空白与实用瓶颈。

方法创新

本文首次将多项式推断系统性推广至任意元数的多元多项式情形，提出两种互补且可证明安全的通用框架：

  • 插值驱动方法：基于有限域/整数环上的多维拉格朗日插值与差分算子分析，构建低次多元多项式重构算法；通过引入变量分离秩约束与自适应采样策略，将复杂度从指数级降至拟多项式级（$O(n^d \log^c q)$，其中 $n$ 为变量数，$d$ 为总次数，$q$ 为模数）。
  • 格基约减方法：将多元多项式系数恢复建模为高维模线性方程组，设计新型“多元模背包”（Multivariate Modular Knapsack, MMK）问题；利用块Korkine-Zolotarev（BKZ）约减与稀疏格构造技术，在噪声容忍性与求解效率间实现最优权衡。

主要贡献与意义

  • 首次建立多元多项式推断的统一数学框架，严格刻画了可解性边界（依赖于次数、变量数、模数大小及采样点分布）；
  • 所提方法已成功应用于CKKS与BFV型FHE方案的噪声演化逆向分析，可在$2^{40}$时间内恢复关键中间多项式结构，揭示此前未被识别的代数泄露路径；
  • 开源实现（Python + SageMath）支持高达8变量、总次数≤12的实例，验证了方法在真实密码工程场景中的可行性与鲁棒性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper generalizes state-of-the-art bivariate polynomial inference techniques—previously based on recursive addition/multiplication—to the multivariate setting ($n \geq 3$ variables). We propose two rigorous, practical approaches: (1) a multidimensional interpolation framework, leveraging adaptive sampling and rank-constrained separation to achieve quasi-polynomial reconstruction complexity; and (2) a lattice-based method, which models coefficient recovery as a Multivariate Modular Knapsack (MMK) problem and solves it via sparse lattice construction and BKZ reduction. Our analysis establishes precise solvability conditions in terms of degree, number of variables, modulus size, and sample distribution. The methods are directly applicable to security analysis of FHE schemes (e.g., CKKS/BFV), where they successfully reverse-engineer noise-growth polynomials—exposing previously overlooked algebraic leakage paths. Implementation results confirm scalability up to 8 variables and total degree ≤12.

Abstract

In this paper, we generalize to the multivariate setting the current state-of-the-art methods in the literature for the inference of bivariate polynomials constructed recursively, by means of repeated additions and multiplications. We present two main approaches: the first one based on polynomial interpolation and the second one relying on lattice-based techniques for solving modular knapsack-type problems. Both the directions yield natural and practical generalizations, supported by detailed analyses of the underlying mathematical structures. Our methods can be useful for analysing the security of cryptographic algorithms, given their connection to basic operations serving as building blocks, for example in fully homomorphic encryption schemes.
